name: API Design Template
content:
```markdown
# API Design / OpenAPI Spec — Template

> **Document status:** Draft | Review | Approved  
> **API name:** `<API Name>`  
> **Version:** `<vX.Y.Z>`  
> **Owner/Team:** `<Team/Org>`  
> **Last updated:** `<YYYY-MM-DD>`  
> **Links:** Repo `<url>`, CI `<url>`, Issue tracker `<url>`, API gateway config `<url>`

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose
- **What this API does:** `<1–2 sentence description>`
- **Primary consumers:** `<internal service / partner / public clients>`
- **Out of scope:** `<what is explicitly not covered>`

### 1.2 Background / Context
- **Problem statement:** `<why this API exists>`
- **Current state:** `<existing systems / prior APIs>`
- **Constraints:** `<regulatory, legacy, performance, timeline>`

### 1.3 Goals & Non-goals
**Goals**
- `<Goal 1>`
- `<Goal 2>`

**Non-goals**
- `<Non-goal 1>`
- `<Non-goal 2>`

### 1.4 Glossary
| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `<Term>` | `<Definition>` |

---

## 2. High-level Design

### 2.1 System Context
- **Upstream systems:** `<list>`
- **Downstream systems:** `<list>`
- **Dependencies:** `<databases, queues, third-party APIs>`

#### Sample context diagram (Mermaid)
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Client[Client App] -->|HTTPS| APIGW[API Gateway]
  APIGW --> API[This API Service]
  API --> DB[(Primary DB)]
  API --> MQ[(Message Queue)]
  API --> EXT[External Provider]
```

### 2.2 Architecture Notes
- **Deployment model:** `<k8s, serverless, VM>`
- **Runtime:** `<language/framework>`
- **Data stores:** `<DB tech + purpose>`
- **Async patterns:** `<events, webhooks, queues>`
- **Idempotency approach:** `<keys, replay handling>`

---

## 3. API Standards & Conventions

### 3.1 Protocol & Format
- **Protocol:** `HTTPS`
- **Style:** `REST` | `GraphQL` | `gRPC`
- **Media types:** `application/json` (default), `<others>`
- **Encoding:** `UTF-8`

### 3.2 URL Structure
- **Base URL (prod):** `https://api.<domain>/<basePath>`
- **Base URL (non-prod):** `https://api-<env>.<domain>/<basePath>`
- **Versioning:** `/v1` in path | header-based | content-negotiation
- **Resource naming:** plural nouns, kebab-case | snake_case

### 3.3 HTTP Semantics
- **GET**: retrieve, no side effects  
- **POST**: create, operations  
- **PUT**: replace  
- **PATCH**: partial update  
- **DELETE**: remove

### 3.4 Pagination, Filtering, Sorting
- **Pagination strategy:** `limit/offset` | `cursor`
- **Example:** `GET /v1/items?limit=50&cursor=abc123`
- **Filtering:** `?status=active&createdAfter=<ISO8601>`
- **Sorting:** `?sort=createdAt,-name`

### 3.5 Idempotency
- **Header:** `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`
- **Scope:** `<per endpoint/per resource>`
- **Retention:** `<e.g., 24h>`

### 3.6 Correlation / Tracing
- **Request ID header:** `X-Request-Id`
- **Trace context:** `traceparent` (W3C)
- **Logging fields:** `<requestId, userId, resourceId, latency>`

---

## 4. Security

### 4.1 Authentication & Authorization
- **Auth method:** OAuth2 | JWT | mTLS | API key
- **Token issuer:** `<url>`
- **Scopes/roles:** `<list>`
- **Example scope mapping:** `<scope> -> <allowed endpoints>`

### 4.2 Transport Security
- TLS minimum version: `<1.2/1.3>`
- Cipher suites: `<policy link>`
- HSTS: `<enabled/disabled>`

### 4.3 Rate Limiting & Quotas
- **Global limit:** `<e.g., 1000 req/min/client>`
- **Burst:** `<e.g., 200 req/10s>`
- **Headers:** `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `Retry-After`

### 4.4 Threat Model / Abuse Cases
- `<enumerate threats: replay, injection, enumeration, SSRF>`
- **Mitigations:** `<validation, WAF rules, authZ checks>`

---

## 5. Data Model

### 5.1 Core Resources
- **Resource:** `<ResourceName>`
  - **Identifier:** `<id type>`
  - **Relationships:** `<belongs to/has many>`
  - **Lifecycle states:** `<draft/active/archived>`

### 5.2 Field Conventions
- **Timestamps:** ISO-8601 UTC (`2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`)
- **Money:** `{ amount: integerMinorUnits, currency: "USD" }` or `<policy>`
- **Nullable vs optional:** `<rules>`
- **Enums:** `<string enums with allowed values>`

### 5.3 Sample JSON Objects
```json
{
  "id": "itm_123",
  "name": "Sample Item",
  "status": "active",
  "createdAt": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

---

## 6. Error Handling

### 6.1 Error Envelope
- **Content-Type:** `application/json`
- **Structure:** `<standard shape across endpoints>`

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "One or more fields are invalid.",
    "details": [
      { "field": "name", "issue": "required" }
    ],
    "requestId": "b3f2c8d0-...."
  }
}
```

### 6.2 HTTP Status Codes
- `200 OK` — success  
- `201 Created` — created  
- `202 Accepted` — async accepted  
- `204 No Content` — success w/o body  
- `400 Bad Request` — invalid input  
- `401 Unauthorized` — missing/invalid auth  
- `403 Forbidden` — insufficient permissions  
- `404 Not Found` — resource not found  
- `409 Conflict` — version/conflict  
- `422 Unprocessable Content` — domain validation  
- `429 Too Many Requests` — rate limit  
- `500/503` — server/unavailable

---

## 7. API Endpoints (Design)

> For each endpoint, fill out the following template.

### 7.1 Endpoint: `<Operation Name>`
- **Method & path:** `GET /v1/<resources>/{id}`
- **Summary:** `<what it does>`
- **Auth:** `<required scopes/roles>`
- **Idempotent:** `<yes/no>`
- **Rate limit bucket:** `<bucket name>`

#### Request
- **Path params**
  - `id` (string) — `<description>`
- **Query params**
  - `<param>` — `<type>` — `<description>`
- **Headers**
  - `X-Request-Id` — `<optional/required>`
- **Body**
```json
{
  "exampleField": "value"
}
```

#### Response
- **Success status:** `200`
- **Body**
```json
{
  "id": "res_123",
  "exampleField": "value"
}
```

#### Error cases
- `400` — `<when>`
- `401` — `<when>`
- `404` — `<when>`

#### Notes
- **Caching:** `<ETag/If-None-Match, max-age>`
- **Consistency:** `<strong/eventual>`
- **Side effects:** `<events published, audits written>`

---

## 8. Async Operations (Optional)

### 8.1 Webhooks
- **Webhook event types:** `<event.created, event.updated>`
- **Delivery:** `<at-least-once / exactly-once?>`
- **Security:** `<HMAC signature header, mTLS>`
- **Retry policy:** `<backoff, max attempts>`

#### Sample webhook payload
```json
{
  "type": "item.created",
  "occurredAt": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "id": "itm_123"
  }
}
```

### 8.2 Events / Messaging
- **Broker:** `<Kafka/SNS/SQS/RabbitMQ>`
- **Topic/queue names:** `<list>`
- **Schema registry:** `<link>`

#### Sample event diagram (Mermaid)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant API as API Service
  participant MQ as Message Broker
  participant C as Consumer
  API->>MQ: Publish item.created
  MQ-->>C: Deliver item.created
  C->>MQ: Ack
```

---

## 9. OpenAPI Specification

### 9.1 OpenAPI Metadata
- **OpenAPI version:** `3.0.3` | `3.1.0`
- **Spec location:** `<path or URL>`
- **Linting:** `<spectral ruleset link>`

### 9.2 Sample OpenAPI YAML (Starter)
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: <API Name>
  version: <vX.Y.Z>
  description: <Short description>
servers:
  - url: https://api.<domain>/<basePath>/v1
paths:
  /items:
    get:
      summary: List items
      operationId: listItems
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: limit
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 100, default: 50 }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ItemListResponse"
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
  schemas:
    Item:
      type: object
      required: [id, name, status, createdAt]
      properties:
        id: { type: string, example: "itm_123" }
        name: { type: string, example: "Sample Item" }
        status: { type: string, enum: [active, inactive] }
        createdAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
    ItemListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Item" }
        nextCursor:
          type: string
          nullable: true
security:
  - bearerAuth: []
```

### 9.3 Generated Artifacts
- **Client SDKs:** `<languages>`  
- **Server stubs:** `<frameworks>`  
- **Docs site:** `<Swagger UI/Redoc link>`  

---

## 10. Validation Rules

### 10.1 Request Validation
- Required fields: `<list>`
- String constraints: `<min/max length, patterns>`
- Numeric constraints: `<min/max>`
- Cross-field rules: `<if A then B>`

### 10.2 Business Rules
- `<rule 1>`
- `<rule 2>`

---

## 11. Compatibility & Versioning

### 11.1 Backward Compatibility Policy
- **Non-breaking changes:** `<add fields, add optional params>`
- **Breaking changes:** `<remove fields, change meaning>`
- **Deprecation window:** `<e.g., 180 days>`

### 11.2 Deprecations
- **Deprecated endpoints/fields:** `<table below>`

| Item | Deprecated in | Removal date | Replacement |
|------|---------------|--------------|-------------|
| `<field/endpoint>` | `<vX.Y>` | `<YYYY-MM-DD>` | `<new>` |

---

## 12. Performance & Reliability

### 12.1 SLIs/SLOs
- **Availability:** `<e.g., 99.9%>`
- **Latency (p95):** `<e.g., 200ms>`
- **Error rate:** `<e.g., <0.1%>`

### 12.2 Capacity / Limits
- Max payload size: `<e.g., 1MB>`
- Max list size: `<e.g., 100 items>`
- Timeouts: `<client/server/gateway>`

### 12.3 Caching Strategy
- **Client caching:** `<ETag/Cache-Control>`
- **Server caching:** `<in-memory/redis>`
- **CDN usage:** `<yes/no>`

---

## 13. Observability & Operations

### 13.1 Logging
- Log format: `<json>`
- PII redaction: `<rules>`
- Sample log fields: `<requestId, actor, route, status, latencyMs>`

### 13.2 Metrics
- Request count, error count, latency histograms
- Rate-limit counters
- Queue lag (if async)

### 13.3 Tracing
- Trace propagation: `<headers>`
- Sampling: `<rate>`

### 13.4 Runbooks
- On-call guide: `<link>`
- Common issues: `<timeouts, auth failures, downstream outage>`

---

## 14. Configuration & Deployment Artifacts (Examples)

### 14.1 Example API Gateway Route (YAML placeholder)
```yaml
routes:
  - name: items
    path: /v1/items
    methods: [GET, POST]
    auth:
      type: oauth2
      scopes: ["items:read", "items:write"]
    rateLimit:
      limitPerMinute: 600
      burst: 50
```

### 14.2 Example Kubernetes Ingress (Optional)
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: <api-name>-ingress
spec:
  rules:
    - host: api-<env>.<domain>
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /<basePath>/v1
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: <api-service>
                port:
                  number: 8080
```

---

## 15. Testing Strategy

### 15.1 Test Types
- Unit tests: `<scope>`
- Integration tests: `<scope>`
- Contract tests: `<OpenAPI-based>`
- Load tests: `<tool + targets>`

### 15.2 Example Requests (cURL)
```bash
curl -sS -X GET "https://api.<domain>/<basePath>/v1/items?limit=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "X-Request-Id: <uuid>"
```

---

## 16. Open Questions / Decisions

### 16.1 Open Questions
- [ ] `<Question 1>`
- [ ] `<Question 2>`

### 16.2 Decision Log (ADR-style)
| Date | Decision | Rationale | Owner |
|------|----------|-----------|-------|
| `<YYYY-MM-DD>` | `<what we decided>` | `<why>` | `<name>` |

---

## Appendix A — Endpoint Inventory (Optional)

| Operation | Method | Path | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|------|------|
| `<listItems>` | GET | `/v1/items` | `items:read` | `<pagination>` |
| `<createItem>` | POST | `/v1/items` | `items:write` | `<idempotency>` |

---

## Appendix B — Changelog
| Version | Date | Change |
|---------|------|--------|
| `<vX.Y.Z>` | `<YYYY-MM-DD>` | `<summary>` |
```

name: NFR Template
content:
# Non-Functional Requirements (NFR) Document

> **Project:** _[Project Name]_  
> **System/Service:** _[System Name]_  
> **Owner:** _[Team/Org]_  
> **Document ID:** _[DOC-###]_  
> **Status:** _Draft | In Review | Approved_  
> **Version:** _vX.Y_  
> **Last Updated:** _YYYY-MM-DD_

---

## 1. Purpose & Scope

### 1.1 Purpose
Describe why this document exists and what it will be used for.  
**Placeholder:** _Define the non-functional requirements (quality attributes, constraints, and operational expectations) for [System Name]._

### 1.2 Scope (In / Out)
- **In scope:** _[e.g., API, web app, background jobs, data pipeline]_
- **Out of scope:** _[e.g., internal tools, legacy systems not being modified]_

### 1.3 Audience
- _[Engineering, SRE, Security, Product, QA, Compliance, Support]_

---

## 2. Definitions, Acronyms, and References

### 2.1 Definitions & Acronyms
| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| SLO | _Service Level Objective_ |
| RTO | _Recovery Time Objective_ |
| RPO | _Recovery Point Objective_ |
| PII | _Personally Identifiable Information_ |

### 2.2 References
- _[Link to architecture doc]_
- _[Link to security standards / policies]_
- _[Link to compliance requirements]_

---

## 3. System Context Overview

### 3.1 High-Level Description
**Placeholder:** _One paragraph summary of the system’s purpose, primary users, and major integrations._

### 3.2 Context Diagram (Recommended)
> Use a diagram to show external systems, users, and major data flows.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  User[End User] -->|HTTPS| Web[Web/App]
  Web --> API[API Service]
  API --> DB[(Primary DB)]
  API --> Cache[(Cache)]
  API --> Ext[External Provider]
  SRE[Ops/SRE] -->|Monitoring| Mon[Observability Stack]
  API --> Mon
```

### 3.3 Key Assumptions & Constraints
- **Assumptions:** _[e.g., cloud provider = AWS; traffic pattern; availability of managed services]_
- **Constraints:** _[e.g., budget limits, tech stack restrictions, regulatory constraints]_

---

## 4. NFR Summary (At-a-Glance)

> Summarize the most important targets in a single table.

| Category | Target | Measurement / Notes |
|----------|--------|---------------------|
| Availability | _99.9% monthly_ | _Measured at API gateway; excludes planned maintenance_ |
| Latency | _p95 < 300ms_ | _For /v1/* endpoints under normal load_ |
| Scalability | _Support 10k RPM_ | _Auto-scale within 5 minutes_ |
| Security | _Encryption in transit + at rest_ | _TLS 1.2+; KMS-managed keys_ |
| Privacy | _PII minimization_ | _Only store fields X, Y; retention = 30 days_ |
| DR | _RTO 2h, RPO 15m_ | _Multi-AZ; backups every 15m_ |

---

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements (Detailed)

> For each NFR: specify **ID, requirement, rationale, priority, metric, test/validation, owner**.

### 5.1 Availability & Reliability

#### 5.1.1 Requirements
| ID | Requirement | Priority | Metric / SLO | Measurement Source | Validation | Owner |
|----|-------------|----------|--------------|--------------------|------------|-------|
| AV-01 | _System must achieve 99.9% availability_ | _P0_ | _≥ 99.9% / month_ | _Uptime checks_ | _SLO report_ | _SRE_ |
| AV-02 | _No single point of failure for critical path_ | _P0_ | _N+1 / Multi-AZ_ | _Architecture review_ | _Game day_ | _Eng_ |

#### 5.1.2 Failure Modes & Resilience (Recommended Table)
| Component | Failure Mode | Expected Behavior | Mitigation |
|----------|--------------|------------------|------------|
| _DB_ | _Primary unavailable_ | _Degraded read-only_ | _Failover / replicas_ |
| _External API_ | _Timeouts_ | _Graceful fallback_ | _Circuit breaker_ |

---

### 5.2 Performance (Latency, Throughput, Resource Use)

#### 5.2.1 Performance Targets
| ID | Area | Requirement | Priority | Target | Conditions | Validation |
|----|------|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------------|
| PF-01 | API latency | _p95 latency_ | _P0_ | _< 300ms_ | _Normal load_ | _Load test_ |
| PF-02 | API throughput | _Sustained RPM_ | _P1_ | _10k RPM_ | _Burst up to 20k for 5 min_ | _Stress test_ |
| PF-03 | Client UX | _First contentful paint_ | _P1_ | _< 2.0s_ | _4G, mid-tier device_ | _RUM_ |

#### 5.2.2 Capacity Model (Optional Diagram)
```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Traffic[Inbound Traffic]
  Traffic --> LB[Load Balancer]
  LB --> ASG[Auto-Scaling Group]
  ASG --> API[API Pods/Instances]
  API --> DB[(DB)]
  API --> Cache[(Cache)]
```

---

### 5.3 Scalability & Elasticity

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------|
| SC-01 | _Scale horizontally without downtime_ | _P0_ | _Zero-downtime rolling deploy_ | _Canary / blue-green_ | _[strategy]_ |
| SC-02 | _Auto-scale on CPU/RPS/queue depth_ | _P1_ | _Scale-out < 5 min_ | _Load test_ | _[policy]_ |

---

### 5.4 Security

#### 5.4.1 Security Requirements
| ID | Requirement | Priority | Standard/Control | Validation | Owner |
|----|-------------|----------|------------------|------------|-------|
| SE-01 | _TLS for all external traffic_ | _P0_ | _TLS 1.2+_ | _Scanner/headers check_ | _Security_ |
| SE-02 | _Least privilege access_ | _P0_ | _IAM roles per service_ | _Access review_ | _Eng_ |
| SE-03 | _Secrets stored in secret manager_ | _P0_ | _No secrets in code_ | _CI scanning_ | _Eng_ |
| SE-04 | _Vulnerability management_ | _P1_ | _SAST/DAST/dependency scanning_ | _Pipeline results_ | _Security_ |

#### 5.4.2 Threat Model (Placeholder)
- **Assets:** _[e.g., user accounts, payment tokens]_
- **Threats:** _[e.g., credential stuffing, injection, SSRF]_
- **Mitigations:** _[rate limiting, WAF, input validation]_

---

### 5.5 Privacy & Compliance

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target/Rule | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|-------------|------------|------|
| PR-01 | _Data minimization_ | _P0_ | _Store only fields: [..]_ | _Design review_ | _[link]_ |
| PR-02 | _Retention & deletion_ | _P0_ | _Delete within 30 days_ | _Audit job_ | _[policy]_ |
| PR-03 | _Compliance_ | _P1_ | _GDPR/CCPA/SOC2/etc._ | _Audit evidence_ | _[scope]_ |

---

### 5.6 Observability (Logging, Metrics, Tracing, Alerting)

#### 5.6.1 Observability Requirements
| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| OB-01 | _Structured logs with correlation IDs_ | _P0_ | _JSON logs; request_id_ | _Log inspection_ |
| OB-02 | _Golden signals metrics_ | _P0_ | _Latency, traffic, errors, saturation_ | _Dashboard review_ |
| OB-03 | _Distributed tracing_ | _P1_ | _Trace ≥ 80% of requests_ | _Tracing report_ |

#### 5.6.2 Alerting & On-Call
| Alert | Trigger | Severity | Response Time | Runbook |
|-------|---------|----------|---------------|---------|
| _High 5xx rate_ | _> 2% for 5m_ | _SEV2_ | _15 min_ | _[link]_ |
| _Latency SLO burn_ | _fast burn_ | _SEV2_ | _15 min_ | _[link]_ |

---

### 5.7 Maintainability & Supportability

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------|
| MA-01 | _Documented runbooks_ | _P0_ | _Top 10 incidents covered_ | _Ops review_ | _[links]_ |
| MA-02 | _Automated CI/CD_ | _P1_ | _Build+test+deploy in < 15 min_ | _Pipeline metrics_ | _[tooling]_ |
| MA-03 | _Backward-compatible APIs_ | _P1_ | _Deprecation policy_ | _API review_ | _[policy]_ |

---

### 5.8 Compatibility & Interoperability

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| CO-01 | _Browser support_ | _P2_ | _Last 2 versions of major browsers_ | _QA matrix_ |
| CO-02 | _API versioning_ | _P1_ | _v1 stable; v2 additive changes_ | _Contract tests_ |

---

### 5.9 Accessibility (If Applicable)

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| AC-01 | _Conform to WCAG_ | _P1_ | _WCAG 2.1 AA_ | _Audit + manual testing_ |

---

### 5.10 Data Management (Integrity, Backups, Archival)

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------|
| DM-01 | _Backups_ | _P0_ | _Every 15 min; retain 30 days_ | _Restore test_ | _[schedule]_ |
| DM-02 | _Data integrity_ | _P0_ | _Constraints + checksums where needed_ | _DB review_ | _[approach]_ |

---

### 5.11 Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------|
| DR-01 | _RTO_ | _P0_ | _≤ 2 hours_ | _DR drill_ | _[region/AZ]_ |
| DR-02 | _RPO_ | _P0_ | _≤ 15 minutes_ | _Backup verification_ | _[replication]_ |

---

### 5.12 Deployment & Release Engineering

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| DE-01 | _Zero-downtime deployments_ | _P0_ | _No user-visible outage_ | _Release checklist_ |
| DE-02 | _Rollback_ | _P0_ | _Rollback < 10 minutes_ | _Game day_ |

---

### 5.13 Cost & Efficiency (FinOps)

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Target | Validation | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|------------|------|
| CO$-01 | _Monthly cloud spend_ | _P2_ | _≤ $[X]/month_ | _Billing dashboards_ | _[allocation]_ |
| CO$-02 | _Cost alerts_ | _P1_ | _Notify at 80/100% budget_ | _Alert test_ | _[owners]_ |

---

## 6. Acceptance Criteria & Verification Plan

### 6.1 Verification Matrix (Recommended)
| NFR ID | Test Type | Tool/Method | Environment | Pass/Fail Criteria | Evidence Link |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|--------------------|--------------|
| AV-01 | _SLO review_ | _[tool]_ | _Prod_ | _≥ 99.9%_ | _[link]_ |
| PF-01 | _Load test_ | _k6/JMeter_ | _Staging_ | _p95 < 300ms_ | _[link]_ |

### 6.2 Exit Criteria for Launch
- _All P0 NFRs verified and signed off_
- _No open SEV1/SEV2 security findings_
- _Runbooks + on-call coverage established_
- _Monitoring/alerts tested_

---

## 7. Risks, Trade-offs, and Decisions

### 7.1 Risks
| Risk | Impact | Likelihood | Mitigation | Owner |
|------|--------|------------|------------|-------|
| _External dependency instability_ | _High_ | _Medium_ | _Circuit breaker + caching_ | _Eng_ |

### 7.2 Trade-offs
- _Example: Higher availability may increase cost due to multi-region replication._

### 7.3 Decisions Log (Optional)
| Date | Decision | Rationale | Approver |
|------|----------|-----------|----------|
| _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _Use Multi-AZ DB_ | _Meet AV-01_ | _[Name]_ |

---

## 8. Open Questions

| Question | Owner | Needed By | Status |
|----------|-------|-----------|--------|
| _What is the agreed SLO measurement boundary?_ | _[Name]_ | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _Open_ |

---

## 9. Approvals

| Role | Name | Approval Date | Notes |
|------|------|---------------|------|
| Product | _[Name]_ | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[optional]_ |
| Engineering | _[Name]_ | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[optional]_ |
| Security | _[Name]_ | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[optional]_ |
| SRE/Operations | _[Name]_ | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[optional]_ |

---

## 10. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Summary of Changes | Reviewers/Approvers |
|---------|------|--------|--------------------|---------------------|
| v0.1 | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[Name]_ | _Initial draft_ | _[Names]_ |
| v0.2 | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[Name]_ | _Added SLOs and DR targets_ | _[Names]_ |
| v1.0 | _YYYY-MM-DD_ | _[Name]_ | _Approved baseline_ | _[Names]_ |

---

## Appendix A: NFR Checklist (Quick Scan)

- [ ] Availability SLO defined (boundary + error budget)
- [ ] Performance targets validated via load/stress tests
- [ ] Security controls mapped to standards
- [ ] Logging/metrics/tracing in place + alerts tested
- [ ] Backup/restore tested; RTO/RPO validated
- [ ] Runbooks created; on-call procedures documented
- [ ] Compliance/privacy requirements confirmed
- [ ] Cost monitoring and budgets configured

> **Owner:** [Name, Role]  
> **Contributors:** [Names/Roles]  
> **Status:** [Draft | In Review | Approved]  
> **Confidentiality:** [Internal | Confidential | Public]

---

## 1. Executive Summary
- **Purpose:** [Why this business architecture is being created]
- **Scope:** [What is included/excluded]
- **Key Outcomes:**  
  - [Outcome 1]  
  - [Outcome 2]
- **Decision Requests (if any):**  
  - [Decision needed + by when + by whom]

---

## 2. Background & Context

## 7. Current State (As-Is) Architecture

flowchart LR

## 8. Target State (To-Be) Architecture

### 8.5 Target Information Architecture

### 8.6 Target Application/Service View (Optional)

---

## 9. Gap Analysis
### 9.1 Capability Gaps
| Capability | As-Is | To-Be | Gap Description | Priority | Proposed Initiative |

## 10. Roadmap & Transition Architecture

gantt

  section Phase 1

## 11. Benefits, KPIs, and Measurement Plan

## 13. Security, Privacy, and Compliance Considerations

name: Database Design / ERD Template
content:
# Database Design / ERD Document Template

> **Project:** [Project Name]  
> **System/Module:** [System or Domain Area]  
> **Author(s):** [Name(s)]  
> **Reviewers:** [Name(s)]  
> **Version:** [0.1]  
> **Last Updated:** [YYYY-MM-DD]  
> **Status:** [Draft | In Review | Approved]  
> **Repository/Link:** [URL]  

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose
- **Goal:** Describe the database design for **[system/module]** to support **[business process]**.
- **In scope:** [e.g., core transactional schema for orders, customers, payments]
- **Out of scope:** [e.g., reporting warehouse, legacy migration]

**Sample placeholder text:**  
This document defines entities, relationships, constraints, indexes, and key design decisions for the **[Product Ordering]** domain.

### 1.2 Audience
- [ ] Backend engineers
- [ ] Data engineers
- [ ] DBAs
- [ ] Product/Business stakeholders

### 1.3 Assumptions & Constraints
- Assumptions:
  - [e.g., Each user has a unique email address]
- Constraints:
  - [e.g., Must support multi-tenant data isolation by `tenant_id`]
- Non-functional requirements:
  - **Availability:** [e.g., 99.9%]
  - **Latency:** [e.g., P95 < 200ms for key reads]
  - **Compliance:** [e.g., GDPR/PII handling]

---

## 2. Requirements Summary

### 2.1 Business Requirements (High-Level)
- [BR-1] [e.g., Users can place orders containing multiple items]
- [BR-2] [e.g., Payments must be auditable and immutable]
- [BR-3] [e.g., Support refunds and partial refunds]

### 2.2 Data Requirements
- Entities to store:
  - [e.g., Users, Orders, OrderItems, Products, Payments]
- Key data access patterns:
  - [e.g., Get user’s orders by date]
  - [e.g., Retrieve order details including items and payment status]

### 2.3 Security / Privacy Requirements
- PII fields: [e.g., email, phone]
- Encryption:
  - At rest: [Yes/No, method]
  - In transit: [TLS]
- Access control:
  - Roles: [e.g., admin, support, user]
  - Row-level isolation: [e.g., by `tenant_id`]

---

## 3. High-Level Data Model

### 3.1 Conceptual ERD (Diagram)
```mermaid
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ ORDER : places
  ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_ITEM : contains
  PRODUCT ||--o{ ORDER_ITEM : referenced_by
  ORDER ||--o{ PAYMENT : has

  USER {
    uuid id PK
    string email UK
    string full_name
  }
  ORDER {
    uuid id PK
    uuid user_id FK
    datetime created_at
    string status
  }
  ORDER_ITEM {
    uuid id PK
    uuid order_id FK
    uuid product_id FK
    int quantity
    decimal unit_price
  }
  PRODUCT {
    uuid id PK
    string sku UK
    string name
    decimal price
  }
  PAYMENT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid order_id FK
    decimal amount
    string provider
    string status
  }
```

**Notes:**
- Replace diagram entities/relationships with your domain entities.
- Keep conceptual ERD focused on major entities and cardinality.

### 3.2 Key Relationships (Summary)
- **USER 1—N ORDER:** A user can place multiple orders.
- **ORDER 1—N ORDER_ITEM:** An order contains multiple line items.
- **PRODUCT 1—N ORDER_ITEM:** A product can appear in multiple orders.
- **ORDER 1—N PAYMENT:** An order can have multiple payment attempts.

---

## 4. Logical Data Model

### 4.1 Entities & Attributes (Logical)
> Use this section to define entities independent of physical DB details.

**Entity: `[EntityName]`**
- Description: [What the entity represents]
- Primary Identifier: `[id]`
- Attributes:
  - `[attribute_1]` — type: [string/int/date], required: [Y/N], description: [...]
  - `[attribute_2]` — type: [...], required: [Y/N], description: [...]
- Relationships:
  - `[EntityName]` 1—N `[OtherEntity]` via `[foreign_key]`

**Sample (Order):**
- Entity: `Order`
- Description: Represents a customer purchase.
- Attributes:
  - `id` (UUID) — required
  - `user_id` (UUID) — required
  - `status` (enum) — required (e.g., `draft|placed|paid|shipped|cancelled`)
  - `created_at` (timestamp) — required
  - `updated_at` (timestamp) — required

### 4.2 Logical ERD (Diagram)
```mermaid
erDiagram
  TENANT ||--o{ USER : owns
  TENANT ||--o{ ORDER : owns
  USER ||--o{ ORDER : places

  TENANT {
    uuid id PK
    string name
  }
  USER {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    string email UK
  }
  ORDER {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid user_id FK
  }
```

**Placeholder notes:**  
If multi-tenancy is not applicable, remove `TENANT` and `tenant_id` references.

---

## 5. Physical Data Model (Schema)

### 5.1 Database Platform & Conventions
- **DBMS:** [PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQL Server/etc.]
- **Schema name(s):** [e.g., `public`, `app`]
- Naming conventions:
  - Tables: [snake_case]
  - Columns: [snake_case]
  - PK: `id`; FK: `[ref_table]_id`
- UUID strategy: [generated by app/db; version]
- Timestamps:
  - `created_at`, `updated_at` [timezone?]
  - Soft delete: `deleted_at` [optional]

### 5.2 Table Definitions (Per Table)
> Provide one subsection per table. Include columns, constraints, and notes.

#### 5.2.1 Table: `[table_name]`
**Purpose:** [What is stored here]  
**Primary Key:** `[id]`  
**Foreign Keys:**  
- `[fk_column]` → `[ref_table].[ref_pk]` (on delete: [cascade/restrict/set null])

**Columns**
| Column | Type | Null | Default | Constraints | Description |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | PK | Unique row identifier |
| created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() |  | Creation timestamp |
| updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() |  | Last update timestamp |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

**Indexes**
- `idx_[table]_[column]` on (`[column]`) — purpose: [query pattern]
- Unique indexes:
  - `uq_[table]_[column]` on (`[column]`)

**Sample mini-diagram (table-level)**
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[(table_name)] -->|FK: fk_column| B[(ref_table)]
```

#### 5.2.2 Table: `orders` (Sample)
**Purpose:** Stores customer orders.  
**Primary Key:** `id`  
**Foreign Keys:** `user_id` → `users.id`

| Column | Type | Null | Default | Constraints | Description |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | PK | Order identifier |
| user_id | uuid | NO |  | FK(users.id) | Owning user |
| status | text | NO | 'placed' | CHECK(status in (...)) | Order lifecycle status |
| total_amount | numeric(12,2) | NO | 0 |  | Cached total (optional) |
| created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() |  | Created time |
| updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() |  | Updated time |

Indexes:
- `idx_orders_user_id_created_at` on (`user_id`, `created_at desc`)
- `idx_orders_status` on (`status`)

---

## 6. Keys, Constraints, and Integrity

### 6.1 Primary & Foreign Keys
- PKs:
  - `[table].id`
- FKs:
  - `[child_table].[fk]` → `[parent_table].id` (ON DELETE [RESTRICT/CASCADE])

### 6.2 Uniqueness & Business Rules
- Unique constraints:
  - `[users.email]` unique
  - `[products.sku]` unique
- Check constraints:
  - `[orders.status]` ∈ {`draft`, `placed`, `paid`, ...}

**Sample constraint snippet (placeholder):**
```sql
-- Example (PostgreSQL)
ALTER TABLE orders
ADD CONSTRAINT chk_orders_status
CHECK (status IN ('draft','placed','paid','shipped','cancelled'));
```

### 6.3 Referential Integrity Notes
- Delete behavior:
  - `orders.user_id` — [restrict] to prevent orphaned orders
  - `order_items.order_id` — [cascade] to delete items when an order is deleted (if allowed)

---

## 7. Normalization & Denormalization Decisions

### 7.1 Normalization Level
- Target: [3NF/BCNF/etc.]
- Justification: [e.g., reduce update anomalies, keep write paths simple]

### 7.2 Denormalizations (If Any)
| Area | Denormalized Field | Source of Truth | Reason | Sync Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| orders | total_amount | order_items | faster reads | recalculated on write |

**Sample note:**  
`orders.total_amount` is derived from `sum(order_items.quantity * unit_price)` and updated in the same transaction as item changes.

---

## 8. Indexing Strategy

### 8.1 Query Patterns
- [Q1] Fetch orders by user, newest first
- [Q2] Retrieve order + items by order id
- [Q3] Search products by SKU

### 8.2 Index Plan
- Composite indexes:
  - `orders(user_id, created_at desc)` for Q1
- Covering indexes: [optional]
- Full-text / trigram: [optional]
- Partitioning: [optional, describe key]

**Sample diagram (access path)**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant API
  participant DB
  API->>DB: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50
  DB-->>API: rows + index scan via idx_orders_user_id_created_at
```

---

## 9. Transaction Boundaries & Concurrency

### 9.1 Transactional Workflows
- **Place Order**
  - Steps:
    1. Insert into `orders`
    2. Insert into `order_items`
    3. (Optional) Update cached totals
  - Isolation level: [read committed / repeatable read / serializable]
  - Expected contention: [low/medium/high]

### 9.2 Concurrency Controls
- Optimistic locking: [e.g., `row_version` or `updated_at` checks]
- Pessimistic locking: [e.g., `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE`]
- Idempotency keys: [for payments/orders]

**Sample pseudo-flow diagram**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Create order request] --> B[Begin TX]
  B --> C[Insert orders]
  C --> D[Insert order_items]
  D --> E[Commit TX]
  E --> F[Return order_id]
```

---

## 10. Data Lifecycle, Retention & Archiving

### 10.1 Retention Policy
- Retain transactional data for: [e.g., 7 years]
- PII retention: [e.g., delete/anonymize after N days post-account closure]

### 10.2 Soft Delete / Hard Delete
- Soft delete: [tables/fields using `deleted_at`]
- Hard delete rules: [what can be deleted and when]

### 10.3 Archiving Strategy
- Approach: [partitioning, archive tables, cold storage]
- Frequency: [daily/weekly/monthly]
- Restore procedure: [brief]

---

## 11. Security Model

### 11.1 Roles & Permissions
- DB roles:
  - `app_readwrite`: [permissions]
  - `app_readonly`: [permissions]
- Principle of least privilege: [notes]

### 11.2 Sensitive Data Handling
- Fields: [e.g., `email`, `address`, `card_token`]
- Masking in logs: [yes/no]
- Encryption approach: [KMS/column encryption/etc.]

**Sample table (classification)**
| Table.Column | Classification | Handling |
|---|---|---|
| users.email | PII | encrypt at rest + limit access |
| payments.provider_ref | sensitive | do not log |

---

## 12. Migration Plan

### 12.1 Schema Migration Tooling
- Tool: [Flyway/Liquibase/Prisma/Alembic/etc.]
- Environments: [dev/stage/prod]
- Rollback strategy: [expand/contract, reversible migrations]

### 12.2 Migration Steps (Sample)
1. Add new table `[new_table]`
2. Backfill data from `[source]`
3. Add constraints/indexes after backfill
4. Cut over application reads/writes
5. Remove deprecated columns/tables

**Sample diagram (expand/contract)**
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Expand: add new columns/tables] --> B[Dual-write / Backfill]
  B --> C[Switch reads]
  C --> D[Contract: drop old columns]
```

---

## 13. Testing & Validation

### 13.1 Data Integrity Tests
- FK constraint tests: [ensure no orphans]
- Uniqueness tests: [ensure unique keys enforced]
- Check constraints: [status enums]

### 13.2 Performance Tests
- Load profile: [reads/writes per second]
- Key queries benchmarked: [list]
- Index effectiveness: [EXPLAIN plans captured]

**Sample placeholder**
- Attach `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)` outputs for:
  - [Query 1]
  - [Query 2]

---

## 14. Open Questions / Risks

### 14.1 Open Questions
- [ ] Should `orders.status` be an enum type or lookup table?
- [ ] Do we need audit tables for all writes?

### 14.2 Risks
- Risk: [e.g., Hot partition on `tenant_id`]
- Mitigation: [e.g., hash partitioning or sharding]

---

## 15. Appendices

### A. Data Dictionary (Optional)
| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Order | A purchase created by a user containing one or more items |
| SKU | Stock keeping unit; unique product identifier |

### B. Full ERD (Optional, Larger Diagram)
```mermaid
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ ORDER : places
  ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_ITEM : contains
  PRODUCT ||--o{ ORDER_ITEM : referenced_by
  ORDER ||--o{ PAYMENT : has
  PAYMENT ||--o{ REFUND : may_have

  REFUND {
    uuid id PK
    uuid payment_id FK
    decimal amount
    datetime created_at
  }
```

### C. Change Log
| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Initial draft |
| 0.2 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Added indexes, constraints |

name: Reference Architecture
content:
# Reference Architecture: [System / Platform Name]

> **Document Version:** 1.0
> **Status:** Draft | In Review | Approved
> **Last Updated:** YYYY-MM-DD
> **Owner:** [Team / Organization Name]
> **Classification:** Public | Internal | Confidential

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary)
2. [Introduction](#2-introduction)
3. [Business Context](#3-business-context)
4. [Architecture Principles](#4-architecture-principles)
5. [System Overview](#5-system-overview)
6. [Logical Architecture](#6-logical-architecture)
7. [Physical Architecture](#7-physical-architecture)
8. [Component Architecture](#8-component-architecture)
9. [Data Architecture](#9-data-architecture)
10. [Integration Architecture](#10-integration-architecture)
11. [Security Architecture](#11-security-architecture)
12. [Infrastructure & Deployment](#12-infrastructure--deployment)
13. [Observability & Operations](#13-observability--operations)
14. [Scalability & Performance](#14-scalability--performance)
15. [Resilience & Disaster Recovery](#15-resilience--disaster-recovery)
16. [Developer Experience](#16-developer-experience)
17. [Cost Architecture](#17-cost-architecture)
18. [Compliance & Governance](#18-compliance--governance)
19. [Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)](#19-architecture-decision-records-adrs)
20. [Risks & Mitigations](#20-risks--mitigations)
21. [Roadmap & Evolution](#21-roadmap--evolution)
22. [Glossary](#22-glossary)
23. [References & Further Reading](#23-references--further-reading)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

> A concise 2-3 paragraph summary of the architecture for a non-technical executive audience.

[System Name] is a [brief description of what the system does] designed to [primary business objective]. This reference architecture describes the recommended approach for building, deploying, and operating [System Name] at scale.

**Key outcomes this architecture enables:**

- [Outcome 1 — e.g., sub-100ms API response times at 10K RPS]
- [Outcome 2 — e.g., 99.99% availability SLA across two regions]
- [Outcome 3 — e.g., SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliance]
- [Outcome 4 — e.g., deployment cycle under 15 minutes]

---

## 2. Introduction

### 2.1 Purpose

This document defines the reference architecture for [System Name]. It is intended to guide implementation teams, serve as a baseline for architectural reviews, and communicate design decisions to stakeholders.

### 2.2 Scope

| In Scope | Out of Scope |
|---|---|
| [Component / layer 1] | [Excluded system 1] |
| [Component / layer 2] | [Excluded system 2] |
| [Deployment environments: dev, staging, prod] | [Legacy system migration details] |
| [Security controls within the platform boundary] | [End-user device management] |

### 2.3 Intended Audience

| Audience | Sections of Interest |
|---|---|
| Engineering Leadership | 1, 3, 17, 21 |
| Solution Architects | All |
| Backend / Platform Engineers | 6, 7, 8, 10, 12 |
| Data Engineers | 9 |
| Security Engineers | 11, 18 |
| SRE / DevOps | 12, 13, 14, 15 |
| Product Managers | 1, 3, 21 |

### 2.4 Document Conventions

- **Bold** — key terms on first use
- `Monospace` — configuration keys, environment variables, CLI commands
- [Placeholder] — values to be filled in per deployment
- *Italic* — notes, caveats, or optional elements

---

## 3. Business Context

### 3.1 Business Drivers

- [Driver 1 — e.g., rapid growth requiring horizontal scalability]
- [Driver 2 — e.g., regulatory requirement to store data in-region]
- [Driver 3 — e.g., reducing time-to-market for new features]

### 3.2 Strategic Goals

| Goal | Description | Success Metric |
|---|---|---|
| [Goal 1] | [Description] | [KPI / measurable target] |
| [Goal 2] | [Description] | [KPI / measurable target] |
| [Goal 3] | [Description] | [KPI / measurable target] |

### 3.3 Stakeholders

| Role | Name / Team | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| Executive Sponsor | [Name] | Final approval, funding |
| Product Owner | [Name] | Requirements, prioritization |
| Architecture Owner | [Name] | Design decisions, ADRs |
| Engineering Lead | [Name] | Implementation oversight |
| Security Lead | [Name] | Security review & sign-off |
| Operations Lead | [Name] | Runbooks, on-call |

### 3.4 Constraints

- **Budget:** [e.g., cloud spend cap of $X/month]
- **Timeline:** [e.g., GA target Q3 YYYY]
- **Technology:** [e.g., must run on AWS, must use Kubernetes]
- **Regulatory:** [e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS]
- **Organizational:** [e.g., team size, existing skill sets]

---

## 4. Architecture Principles

> These principles guide all design decisions. Any deviation requires an ADR.

| # | Principle | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| P1 | **Design for failure** | Assume any component can fail at any time; build resiliency in. |
| P2 | **Security by default** | Least privilege, encryption at rest and in transit, zero trust. |
| P3 | **API-first** | All capabilities exposed via versioned APIs; no direct DB access across service boundaries. |
| P4 | **Automate everything** | Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD pipelines, automated testing. |
| P5 | **Observability over debugging** | Structured logs, distributed tracing, and metrics from day one. |
| P6 | **Prefer managed services** | Use cloud-managed services to reduce operational overhead unless there is a strong reason not to. |
| P7 | **Loose coupling, high cohesion** | Services own their data; communicate via well-defined contracts. |
| P8 | **Optimize for change** | Design for extensibility; avoid big-bang rewrites. |

---

## 5. System Overview

### 5.1 Context Diagram (C4 Level 1)

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context — [System Name]

    Person(user, "End User", "A user of the platform via web or mobile")
    Person(admin, "Administrator", "Manages configuration and monitors the system")

    System(system, "[System Name]", "Core platform providing [primary capability]")

    System_Ext(idp, "Identity Provider", "e.g., Auth0, Okta, Azure AD")
    System_Ext(payment, "Payment Gateway", "e.g., Stripe, Braintree")
    System_Ext(notifications, "Notification Service", "e.g., SendGrid, Twilio")
    System_Ext(analytics, "Analytics Platform", "e.g., Segment, Mixpanel")

    Rel(user, system, "Uses", "HTTPS")
    Rel(admin, system, "Manages", "HTTPS")
    Rel(system, idp, "Authenticates via", "OAuth 2.0 / OIDC")
    Rel(system, payment, "Processes payments via", "HTTPS / REST")
    Rel(system, notifications, "Sends notifications via", "HTTPS / REST")
    Rel(system, analytics, "Streams events to", "HTTPS")
```

### 5.2 Key Capabilities

- **[Capability 1]:** [Brief description]
- **[Capability 2]:** [Brief description]
- **[Capability 3]:** [Brief description]
- **[Capability 4]:** [Brief description]

### 5.3 Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)

| Category | Requirement | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Availability | Uptime SLA | 99.9% / 99.99% |
| Latency | p99 API response time | < [X] ms |
| Throughput | Peak requests per second | [X] RPS |
| Data Volume | Storage growth per month | [X] GB/month |
| Recovery Time | RTO (Recovery Time Objective) | < [X] minutes |
| Recovery Point | RPO (Recovery Point Objective) | < [X] minutes |
| Scalability | Horizontal scale trigger | CPU > [X]% or queue depth > [X] |
| Security | Auth standard | OAuth 2.0 + OIDC |
| Compliance | Regulatory frameworks | [GDPR / HIPAA / SOC 2 / PCI] |

---

## 6. Logical Architecture

### 6.1 Logical Layers

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Presentation["Presentation Layer"]
        WEB[Web App]
        MOB[Mobile App]
        CLI[CLI / SDK]
    end

    subgraph Gateway["Gateway Layer"]
        APIGW[API Gateway]
        WAF[WAF / DDoS Protection]
        CDN[CDN]
    end

    subgraph Application["Application Layer"]
        SVC1[Service A]
        SVC2[Service B]
        SVC3[Service C]
        SVC4[Service D]
    end

    subgraph Messaging["Messaging Layer"]
        MQ[Message Broker\ne.g. Kafka / RabbitMQ]
    end

    subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
        DB1[(Primary DB\ne.g. PostgreSQL)]
        DB2[(Cache\ne.g. Redis)]
        DB3[(Search\ne.g. Elasticsearch)]
        DB4[(Object Store\ne.g. S3)]
    end

    subgraph Platform["Platform Layer"]
        AUTH[Auth Service]
        LOG[Logging]
        TRACE[Tracing]
        CONFIG[Config Management]
    end

    Presentation --> Gateway
    Gateway --> Application
    Application --> Messaging
    Application --> Data
    Application --> Platform
    Messaging --> Application
```

### 6.2 Domain Decomposition

| Domain | Responsibilities | Owning Team |
|---|---|---|
| [Domain 1 — e.g., Identity] | User registration, authentication, authorization | [Team Name] |
| [Domain 2 — e.g., Catalog] | Product/content management, search | [Team Name] |
| [Domain 3 — e.g., Orders] | Order lifecycle, fulfillment | [Team Name] |
| [Domain 4 — e.g., Billing] | Payments, invoices, subscriptions | [Team Name] |
| [Domain 5 — e.g., Notifications] | Email, SMS, push delivery | [Team Name] |

---

## 7. Physical Architecture

### 7.1 Deployment Topology

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph Internet
        USR[Users]
    end

    subgraph CloudProvider["Cloud Provider (e.g., AWS / GCP / Azure)"]
        subgraph Region1["Primary Region (e.g., us-east-1)"]
            subgraph AZ1["Availability Zone A"]
                APP1[App Nodes]
                DB1[(DB Primary)]
            end
            subgraph AZ2["Availability Zone B"]
                APP2[App Nodes]
                DB2[(DB Replica)]
            end
            LB[Load Balancer]
            CDN1[CDN Edge]
        end

        subgraph Region2["DR Region (e.g., us-west-2)"]
            APP3[App Nodes - Standby]
            DB3[(DB Replica - DR)]
        end
    end

    USR --> CDN1
    CDN1 --> LB
    LB --> APP1
    LB --> APP2
    APP1 --> DB1
    APP2 --> DB1
    DB1 --> DB2
    DB1 --> DB3
```

### 7.2 Environment Strategy

| Environment | Purpose | Scale | Data | Access |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `development` | Local dev & unit testing | Minimal | Synthetic | Developer |
| `integration` | Integration & contract testing | Small | Synthetic | Developer + CI |
| `staging` | Pre-prod validation, load testing | Production-like | Anonymized | Dev + QA + PMs |
| `production` | Live traffic | Full | Real | Ops + automated |
| `dr` | Disaster recovery failover | Warm standby | Replicated | Ops only |

---

## 8. Component Architecture

### 8.1 Component Diagram (C4 Level 2)

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Container Diagram — [System Name]

    Person(user, "User")

    Container(spa, "Web SPA", "React / Vue / Angular", "Browser-based UI")
    Container(mobile, "Mobile App", "iOS / Android", "Native or cross-platform app")
    Container(apigw, "API Gateway", "e.g., Kong / AWS API GW", "Rate limiting, auth, routing")
    Container(svcA, "Service A", "Node.js / Go / Python", "Handles [domain A] logic")
    Container(svcB, "Service B", "Node.js / Go / Python", "Handles [domain B] logic")
    Container(worker, "Background Worker", "e.g., Celery / BullMQ", "Async job processing")
    ContainerDb(db, "Primary Database", "PostgreSQL / MongoDB", "Persistent application state")
    ContainerDb(cache, "Cache", "Redis", "Session store, hot data cache")
    Container(broker, "Message Broker", "Kafka / RabbitMQ", "Async event bus")

    Rel(user, spa, "Uses", "HTTPS")
    Rel(user, mobile, "Uses")
    Rel(spa, apigw, "API calls", "HTTPS / REST / GraphQL")
    Rel(mobile, apigw, "API calls", "HTTPS / REST")
    Rel(apigw, svcA, "Routes to", "HTTP / gRPC")
    Rel(apigw, svcB, "Routes to", "HTTP / gRPC")
    Rel(svcA, db, "Reads/Writes")
    Rel(svcA, cache, "Reads/Writes")
    Rel(svcA, broker, "Publishes events")
    Rel(broker, worker, "Consumes events")
    Rel(worker, db, "Reads/Writes")
```

### 8.2 Service Inventory

| Service | Language / Runtime | Protocol | Owns Data Store | Scales To |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Service A] | [e.g., Go 1.22] | REST / HTTP | PostgreSQL (schema: `svc_a`) | [X] replicas |
| [Service B] | [e.g., Node.js 20] | gRPC | MongoDB (db: `svc_b`) | [X] replicas |
| [Worker] | [e.g., Python 3.12] | Queue consumer | - | [X] replicas |
| [Scheduler] | [e.g., Go 1.22] | Cron / internal | PostgreSQL (schema: `jobs`) | 1 (leader-elected) |

### 8.3 API Design Standards

- **Style:** REST / GraphQL / gRPC — [choose one, justify in ADR]
- **Versioning:** URI path versioning — `/v1/`, `/v2/`
- **Auth:** Bearer token (JWT) via `Authorization` header
- **Content-Type:** `application/json`
- **Pagination:** Cursor-based (`after`, `before`, `limit`)
- **Error format:** RFC 7807 Problem Details
- **Rate limiting:** [X] req/min per API key / user

---

## 9. Data Architecture

### 9.1 Data Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    INGEST[Data Ingestion\nAPI / Events / ETL]
    OLTP[(OLTP Store\nPostgreSQL / MongoDB)]
    STREAM[Stream Processing\nKafka / Kinesis]
    DWH[(Data Warehouse\nSnowflake / BigQuery / Redshift)]
    LAKE[(Data Lake\nS3 / GCS)]
    ANALYTICS[BI / Analytics\nLooker / Metabase / Superset]
    ML[ML Platform\nSageMaker / Vertex AI]

    INGEST --> OLTP
    OLTP --> STREAM
    STREAM --> DWH
    STREAM --> LAKE
    DWH --> ANALYTICS
    LAKE --> ML
    ML --> OLTP
```

### 9.2 Data Store Selection

| Store | Technology | Use Case | Consistency | Scale Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Primary DB | [PostgreSQL 16] | Transactional application data | Strong (ACID) | Read replicas + connection pooling (PgBouncer) |
| Cache | [Redis 7] | Sessions, hot data, rate limit counters | Eventual | Redis Cluster |
| Search | [Elasticsearch 8] | Full-text search, faceted filtering | Eventual | Shard per index |
| Object Store | [S3 / GCS] | Blobs, media, backups, exports | Eventual | Infinite (managed) |
| Time Series | [InfluxDB / TimescaleDB] | Metrics, IoT, telemetry | Eventual | Retention policies |
| Data Warehouse | [Snowflake / BigQuery] | Analytics, reporting | Eventual | Warehouse scaling |

### 9.3 Data Governance

- **Data Classification:** [Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted]
- **PII Handling:** [Describe anonymization / pseudonymization approach]
- **Data Residency:** [Regions where data must/must not reside]
- **Retention Policy:** [e.g., 90 days hot, 1 year warm, 7 years cold archive]
- **Data Lineage:** [Tool used — e.g., OpenLineage, DataHub]
- **Master Data Management:** [Approach — e.g., golden record service]

### 9.4 Schema Management

- **Migrations tool:** [Flyway / Liquibase / Alembic / golang-migrate]
- **Strategy:** Forward-only migrations; no destructive changes without deprecation period
- **Review process:** Schema changes require architecture review if they affect > 1 service

---

## 10. Integration Architecture

### 10.1 Integration Patterns Used

| Pattern | When Used | Technology |
|---|---|---|
| Synchronous REST | Request/response, simple queries | HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2 |
| gRPC | High-performance internal service calls | gRPC + Protobuf |
| Async Messaging | Event-driven workflows, decoupling | Kafka / RabbitMQ / SQS |
| Webhooks | Outbound notifications to third parties | HTTPS POST |
| Batch / ETL | Bulk data transfer to warehouse | Airflow / dbt |
| GraphQL | Flexible client-driven queries | Apollo / Hasura |

### 10.2 Event Catalog (Sample)

| Event Name | Producer | Consumer(s) | Schema | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `user.registered` | Auth Service | Notifications, Analytics | [Link to schema] | ~[X]/day |
| `order.placed` | Order Service | Billing, Fulfillment, Analytics | [Link to schema] | ~[X]/day |
| `payment.completed` | Billing Service | Order, Notifications | [Link to schema] | ~[X]/day |
| `[event.name]` | [Service] | [Service(s)] | [Link] | ~[X]/day |

### 10.3 Third-Party Integrations

| Integration | Purpose | Protocol | Auth | SLA / Rate Limits |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Auth0 / Okta] | Identity & SSO | OIDC / OAuth 2.0 | Client credentials | [X] req/s |
| [Stripe] | Payments | REST | API Key | [X] req/s |
| [SendGrid / SES] | Email delivery | REST | API Key | [X] emails/day |
| [Datadog / New Relic] | Observability | Agent / REST | API Key | N/A |
| [PagerDuty] | Incident alerting | REST / Webhook | API Key | N/A |

---

## 11. Security Architecture

### 11.1 Security Layers

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Perimeter["Perimeter Security"]
        WAF[WAF]
        DDOS[DDoS Protection]
        CDN[CDN / Edge]
    end

    subgraph Network["Network Security"]
        FW[Firewall / Security Groups]
        VPC[Private VPC / VNet]
        TLS[TLS 1.2+ Everywhere]
    end

    subgraph Identity["Identity & Access"]
        IDP[Identity Provider]
        RBAC[RBAC / ABAC]
        MFA[MFA Enforcement]
        SECRETS[Secrets Manager]
    end

    subgraph Application["Application Security"]
        AUTHN[Authentication\nJWT / OAuth 2.0]
        AUTHZ[Authorization\nOPA / Casbin]
        VALID[Input Validation]
        AUDIT[Audit Logging]
    end

    subgraph Data["Data Security"]
        ENC_REST[Encryption at Rest\nAES-256]
        ENC_TRANSIT[Encryption in Transit\nTLS 1.3]
        DLP[Data Loss Prevention]
        MASK[PII Masking / Tokenization]
    end

    Perimeter --> Network
    Network --> Identity
    Identity --> Application
    Application --> Data
```

### 11.2 Authentication & Authorization

- **AuthN standard:** OAuth 2.0 + OIDC
- **Token type:** Short-lived JWT (access: 15 min, refresh: 7 days)
- **MFA:** Required for admin roles; optional for standard users
- **Session management:** Stateless via JWT; revocation via token blocklist in Redis
- **AuthZ model:** RBAC with attribute-based extensions (ABAC) for fine-grained rules
- **Policy engine:** [OPA / Casbin / built-in]

### 11.3 Secrets Management

- **Tool:** [HashiCorp Vault / AWS Secrets Manager / GCP Secret Manager]
- **Rotation:** Automated rotation every [X] days
- **No secrets in:** Source code, Docker images, environment variable files committed to git
- **Access:** Service accounts with minimal scope; audited

### 11.4 Security Controls Checklist

| Control | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| TLS 1.2+ on all endpoints | [Done / Planned] | |
| Secrets manager (no plaintext secrets) | [Done / Planned] | |
| Dependency vulnerability scanning | [Done / Planned] | e.g., Snyk, Dependabot |
| Container image scanning | [Done / Planned] | e.g., Trivy, Grype |
| SAST in CI pipeline | [Done / Planned] | e.g., Semgrep, SonarQube |
| DAST / Pen test | [Done / Planned] | Frequency: [Annually / Quarterly] |
| Audit logging (who did what, when) | [Done / Planned] | |
| Data encryption at rest | [Done / Planned] | AES-256 |
| Incident response plan | [Done / Planned] | Link: [runbook URL] |
| WAF rules tuned | [Done / Planned] | |

---

## 12. Infrastructure & Deployment

### 12.1 Infrastructure Stack

| Layer | Technology | Managed By |
|---|---|---|
| Cloud Provider | [AWS / GCP / Azure / Multi-cloud] | [Cloud team] |
| Container Orchestration | [Kubernetes / ECS / Cloud Run] | [Platform team] |
| Service Mesh | [Istio / Linkerd / None] | [Platform team] |
| Infrastructure as Code | [Terraform / Pulumi / CDK] | [Platform team] |
| Container Registry | [ECR / GCR / Docker Hub] | [Platform team] |
| DNS | [Route 53 / Cloud DNS / Cloudflare] | [Platform team] |
| Load Balancer | [ALB / GCP LB / Nginx] | [Platform team] |
| CDN | [CloudFront / Fastly / Cloudflare] | [Platform team] |

### 12.2 CI/CD Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    DEV[Developer\nPush to branch] --> PR[Pull Request\nCode Review]
    PR --> CI

    subgraph CI["CI Pipeline"]
        LINT[Lint & Format]
        TEST[Unit & Integration Tests]
        SCAN[Security Scan\nSAST + SCA]
        BUILD[Build Docker Image]
        PUSH[Push to Registry]
    end

    CI --> CD

    subgraph CD["CD Pipeline"]
        DEP_STG[Deploy to Staging]
        SMOKE[Smoke Tests]
        APPROVE{Manual Approval\nfor Prod?}
        DEP_PROD[Deploy to Production]
        CANARY[Canary / Blue-Green]
        ROLLBACK[Auto Rollback\non failure]
    end

    DEP_STG --> SMOKE --> APPROVE --> DEP_PROD --> CANARY --> ROLLBACK
```

### 12.3 Deployment Strategy

| Strategy | Used For | Rollback Time |
|---|---|---|
| Rolling update | Standard deploys | ~[X] min |
| Blue/Green | Major releases, DB migrations | ~[X] min (instant switch) |
| Canary | High-risk changes | Gradual, monitor hours |
| Feature flags | Dark launches, A/B tests | Instant (flag toggle) |

### 12.4 Infrastructure as Code Structure

```
infrastructure/
├── modules/
│   ├── networking/       # VPC, subnets, security groups
│   ├── compute/          # EKS/GKE cluster, node pools
│   ├── database/         # RDS, CloudSQL, managed DB
│   ├── storage/          # S3, GCS buckets
│   └── observability/    # Log sinks, monitoring
├── environments/
│   ├── dev/
│   ├── staging/
│   └── production/
└── global/               # DNS, CDN, IAM roles
```

---

## 13. Observability & Operations

### 13.1 Observability Pillars

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Signals["Three Pillars of Observability"]
        LOGS[Logs\nStructured JSON\ne.g. Loki / CloudWatch / ELK]
        METRICS[Metrics\nPrometheus / CloudWatch\nDashboards: Grafana]
        TRACES[Distributed Traces\nOpenTelemetry\ne.g. Jaeger / Tempo / Datadog]
    end

    subgraph Alerting["Alerting & Incident Management"]
        ALERT[Alertmanager / PagerDuty]
        RUNBOOK[Runbooks]
        ONCALL[On-Call Rotation]
    end

    Signals --> Alerting
```

### 13.2 Key Metrics (Golden Signals)

| Signal | Metric | Alert Threshold | Dashboard |
|---|---|---|---|
| Latency | p50 / p95 / p99 API response time | p99 > [X]ms for 5 min | [Link] |
| Traffic | Requests per second | Anomaly detection | [Link] |
| Errors | 5xx error rate | > [X]% for 2 min | [Link] |
| Saturation | CPU / Memory / Queue depth | CPU > [X]% for 10 min | [Link] |

### 13.3 Log Management

- **Format:** Structured JSON with fields: `timestamp`, `level`, `service`, `trace_id`, `span_id`, `message`, `context`
- **Collection:** [Fluentd / Vector / CloudWatch agent]
- **Storage:** [Loki / Elasticsearch / CloudWatch Logs]
- **Retention:** [X] days hot, [X] days warm, archived to cold storage
- **Sensitive data:** PII must be masked before logging

### 13.4 SLI / SLO / SLA Framework

| Service | SLI | SLO | SLA |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway | Request success rate | 99.9% over 30 days | 99.5% |
| [Service A] | p99 latency < [X]ms | 99.5% of requests | 99.0% |
| [Database] | Query success rate | 99.95% | 99.9% |

---

## 14. Scalability & Performance

### 14.1 Scaling Strategy

| Component | Scaling Type | Trigger | Min | Max |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway | Horizontal | RPS > [X] | [N] | [N] |
| [Service A] | Horizontal | CPU > [X]% | [N] | [N] |
| [Service B] | Horizontal | Queue depth > [X] | [N] | [N] |
| Database | Vertical + Read replicas | Manual | 1 primary | [N] replicas |
| Cache | Cluster | Memory > [X]% | [N] nodes | [N] nodes |

### 14.2 Performance Targets

| Scenario | Target | Test Method |
|---|---|---|
| Steady state [X] RPS | p99 < [X]ms | Load test (k6 / Gatling) |
| 3x traffic spike | No errors, p99 < [X]ms | Spike test |
| Database failover | < [X]s downtime | Chaos test |
| Cold start (scale from 0) | < [X]s to first request | Integration test |

### 14.3 Caching Strategy

| Cache Layer | Technology | TTL | Invalidation Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|
| CDN / Edge | [CloudFront / Cloudflare] | [X] min | Cache-Control headers, manual purge |
| API response | [Redis] | [X] sec | Event-driven (on data change) |
| DB query | [Redis] | [X] sec | TTL expiry + write-through |
| Static assets | [CDN] | 1 year | Content hash in filename |

---

## 15. Resilience & Disaster Recovery

### 15.1 Failure Modes & Mitigations

| Failure Scenario | Impact | Mitigation | Recovery |
|---|---|---|---|
| Single AZ outage | Partial degradation | Multi-AZ deployment | Automatic (LB re-routes) |
| Primary DB failure | Write unavailable | Automated failover to replica | < [X] min |
| Upstream API unavailable | Feature degradation | Circuit breaker + fallback | Graceful degradation |
| Memory leak in service | Increased latency | Liveness probes + auto-restart | < [X] min |
| DDoS attack | Service unavailable | WAF + rate limiting + CDN | Automatic |
| Full region outage | Total outage | DR region warm standby | < [RTO] min |
| Data corruption | Data loss | Point-in-time backups | < [RPO] data loss |

### 15.2 Resilience Patterns

- **Circuit Breaker:** [Resilience4j / Polly / go-resiliency] — trip after [X]% errors in [X]s window
- **Retry with backoff:** Exponential backoff, max [X] retries, jitter added
- **Bulkhead:** Separate thread pools / connection pools per downstream
- **Timeout:** All outbound calls have explicit timeouts ([X]ms default)
- **Rate limiting:** Token bucket at API gateway + per-service limits
- **Health checks:** Liveness (`/healthz`) and readiness (`/readyz`) endpoints on all services

### 15.3 Backup & Recovery

| Data Store | Backup Frequency | Retention | Recovery Method | Test Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Primary DB | Continuous WAL + daily snapshot | 30 days | PITR or snapshot restore | Monthly |
| Object Store | Cross-region replication | 90 days | Restore from replica | Quarterly |
| Config / Secrets | Version-controlled + encrypted backup | Indefinite | Re-apply IaC | On change |

---

## 16. Developer Experience

### 16.1 Local Development Setup

```bash
# Clone and bootstrap
git clone [repo-url]
cd [project]
cp .env.example .env

# Start local stack (docker-compose or devcontainer)
docker compose up -d

# Run migrations
[migration-command]

# Start services
[start-command]

# Run tests
[test-command]
```

### 16.2 Repository Structure

```
[project-root]/
├── services/
│   ├── service-a/
│   ├── service-b/
│   └── worker/
├── packages/              # Shared libraries / SDKs
├── infrastructure/        # Terraform / Pulumi IaC
├── .github/workflows/     # CI/CD pipeline definitions
├── docs/                  # Architecture docs, ADRs, runbooks
├── scripts/               # Dev tooling scripts
├── docker-compose.yml     # Local development stack
└── Makefile               # Common commands
```

### 16.3 Tooling Standards

| Category | Tool | Version |
|---|---|---|
| Version Control | Git + GitHub / GitLab | - |
| Package Manager | [npm / pnpm / cargo / go modules] | [version] |
| Linter | [ESLint / golangci-lint / Ruff] | [version] |
| Formatter | [Prettier / gofmt / Black] | [version] |
| Test Framework | [Jest / pytest / Go testing] | [version] |
| API Testing | [Postman / Bruno / Insomnia] | [version] |
| IaC | [Terraform / Pulumi] | [version] |
| Container | Docker + [Compose / Podman] | [version] |

---

## 17. Cost Architecture

### 17.1 Cost Breakdown (Estimated)

| Category | Service | Est. Monthly Cost | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Compute | [EKS nodes / GKE] | $[X] | [X] nodes, [instance type] |
| Database | [RDS / CloudSQL] | $[X] | [instance type] + storage |
| Cache | [ElastiCache / Memorystore] | $[X] | [X] nodes |

| Observability | [Datadog / New Relic] | $[X] | [X] hosts |
| **Total** | | **$[X]/month** | |

### 17.2 Cost Optimization Strategies

- **Spot / Preemptible instances** for non-critical workers and batch jobs
- **Auto-scaling** to zero during off-peak hours for non-prod environments
- **Reserved instances / committed use discounts** for steady-state workloads
- **Data lifecycle policies** to tier storage from hot to cold
- **Right-sizing** reviews on a [quarterly] basis using [Cost Explorer / Recommender]

---

## 18. Compliance & Governance

### 18.1 Regulatory Requirements

| Regulation | Applicability | Key Controls | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| GDPR | [Yes / No — reason] | Data subject rights, DPA, breach notification | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| HIPAA | [Yes / No — reason] | PHI encryption, BAAs, audit logs | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| PCI-DSS | [Yes / No — reason] | No card data stored; tokenization via Stripe | [Compliant / In Progress] |

### 18.2 Architecture Governance

- **Architecture Review Board (ARB):** Reviews significant changes; meets [bi-weekly / monthly]
- **ADR Process:** All significant decisions documented as ADRs (see Section 19)

## 19. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

> ADRs capture the context, decision, and consequences of significant architectural choices.

**Negative:** [Trade-offs or downsides]
**Neutral:** [Other notable effects]
```

| ADR-003 | [e.g., Use JWT for stateless authentication] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |

content:
