- [ ] Consumer group sizing validated against lag SLA
- [ ] API gateway rate limits set below backend capacity
- [ ] Connection pool sizes configured per downstream service
- [ ] Load test executed against staging at [X]x expected prod peak

---

## 26. Compliance & Regulatory Requirements

### 26.1 Regulatory Requirements for Integrations

| Regulation | Applicable? | Impact on Integrations | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| GDPR | [Yes] | PII minimization in transit; no PII in logs; right to erasure propagation | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| CCPA | [Yes] | PII handling in API responses; opt-out propagation to downstream systems | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| PCI-DSS | [Yes — payments] | No card data in platform; tokenize before sending; scope isolation | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| HIPAA | [Yes / No] | PHI encryption in transit + at rest; BAA with SaaS vendors | [Compliant / In Progress] |
| SOC 2 Type II | [Yes] | Audit logging of all integration access; availability SLAs | [Compliant / In Progress] |

### 26.2 Data Residency Requirements

| Data Domain | Allowed Regions | Restricted From | Enforcement Mechanism |
|---|---|---|---|
| EU Customer PII | EU regions only | US, APAC | API gateway geo-routing; DPA with SaaS vendors |
| Financial data | [Region] | [Excluded regions] | Network policy; DPA |
| [Domain] | [Regions] | [Restricted] | [Mechanism] |

### 26.3 Third-Party Vendor Compliance

| Vendor | Data Shared | DPA Signed | Last Security Review | Certifications |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Salesforce | Customer PII, contacts | Yes | [Date] | SOC 2, ISO 27001 |
| Stripe | Payment metadata (no raw card data) | Yes | [Date] | PCI-DSS Level 1 |
| SendGrid | Email address, message content | Yes | [Date] | SOC 2 |
| [Vendor] | [Data] | [Yes/No] | [Date] | [Certs] |

---

## 27. Cost Management

### 27.1 Integration Cost Breakdown (Estimated)

| Category | Service | Est. Monthly Cost | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway | [Kong / AWS APIGW / Apigee] | $[X] | Per [million requests / node] |
| Kafka / Messaging | [Confluent Cloud / MSK] | $[X] | [X] partitions, [X] GB/day |
| iPaaS Platform | [MuleSoft / Boomi / Logic Apps] | $[X] | Per [connector / execution / vcore] |
| Service Mesh | [Istio on K8s / Managed] | $[X] | Included in compute or separate |
| MFT / SFTP | [AWS Transfer Family / GoAnywhere] | $[X] | Per [protocol endpoint + GB] |
| Developer Portal | [ReadMe / Backstage hosting] | $[X] | Per [seat / tier] |
| Observability (tracing) | [Datadog APM / Jaeger] | $[X] | Per [host / ingested span GB] |
| **Total** | | **$[X]/month** | |

### 27.2 Cost Optimization Strategies

- **API Gateway:** Cache responses at gateway for read-heavy endpoints; reduce backend invocations
- **Kafka:** Right-size partition count; use log compaction for reference topics; tiered storage for long retention
- **iPaaS:** Consolidate low-volume flows; use serverless triggers instead of always-on workers
- **Observability:** Sample high-volume low-error traces; reduce cardinality on metric labels
- **MFT:** Batch small files; compress before transfer

---

## 28. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

### ADR Template

```
# ADR-[NNN]: [Short Title]

Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Status: Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded by ADR-[NNN]
Deciders: [Names / roles]

## Context
[What is the situation or problem requiring a decision?]

## Options Considered
1. [Option A] — [brief description + trade-offs]
2. [Option B] — [brief description + trade-offs]

## Decision
[What was chosen and why?]

## Consequences
Positive: [Benefits]
Negative: [Trade-offs or downsides]
Neutral: [Other effects]
```

### ADR Index

| ADR | Title | Date | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADR-001 | [e.g., Adopt API-first design with OpenAPI specs] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-002 | [e.g., Use Kafka over RabbitMQ as primary event broker] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-003 | [e.g., Adopt Kong as API gateway] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-004 | [e.g., Use Pact for consumer-driven contract testing] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-005 | [e.g., Adopt Istio service mesh for internal traffic] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-006 | [e.g., Replace legacy ESB with iPaaS + event bus] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-007 | [e.g., Use CloudEvents spec for all event envelopes] | YYYY-MM-DD | Accepted |
| ADR-008 | [Title] | YYYY-MM-DD | [Status] |

---

## 29. Risks & Mitigations

| ID | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Severity | Mitigation | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| R01 | API gateway as single point of failure | Low | Critical | High | Multi-AZ; auto-scaling; health checks; bypass routing for internal | [Platform] |
| R02 | Kafka broker outage disrupts all async integrations | Low | High | High | Multi-AZ Kafka; replication factor 3; consumer DLQ + replay | [Platform] |
| R03 | Schema change in producer breaks consumers silently | High | High | High | Schema registry + BACKWARD compatibility; contract tests in CI | [All teams] |
| R04 | Third-party SaaS API unavailable (e.g., Stripe) | Medium | Critical | High | Circuit breaker; queue and retry; graceful degradation | [Service owner] |
| R05 | Credentials / API keys exposed in logs or code | Medium | Critical | High | Secrets manager; log scrubbing; pre-commit secret scanning | [Security] |
| R06 | Partner sends malformed or malicious data | Medium | High | High | Input validation at gateway; iPaaS schema check; quarantine | [Integration] |
| R07 | Deprecated API not retired → zombie integrations | High | Medium | Medium | `Sunset` headers; gateway analytics; forced retirement process | [API owners] |
| R08 | iPaaS vendor lock-in | Low | High | Medium | Abstract flows behind canonical model; evaluate alternatives yearly | [Arch] |
| R09 | Integration test gaps — breaking change in prod | Medium | High | High | Contract tests required for all consumers; CI gate | [All teams] |
| R10 | [Risk] | [L/M/H] | [L/M/H/Crit] | [L/M/H] | [Mitigation] | [Owner] |

---

## 30. Roadmap & Evolution

### 30.1 Current vs Target State

| Dimension | Current State | Target State (12 months) |
|---|---|---|
| API design | [e.g., ad-hoc, no standard] | [e.g., API-first with OpenAPI for all new APIs] |
| Event architecture | [e.g., direct service calls] | [e.g., event-driven with Kafka for all async workflows] |
| API gateway | [e.g., Nginx reverse proxy only] | [e.g., Kong with auth, rate limiting, developer portal] |
| Service mesh | [e.g., none] | [e.g., Istio with mTLS and traffic policies] |
| Contract testing | [e.g., none] | [e.g., Pact covering all consumer/producer pairs] |
| Partner integrations | [e.g., manual file exchange] | [e.g., managed partner portal with self-service onboarding] |
| Observability | [e.g., basic logging] | [e.g., full distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry] |
| Legacy ESB | [e.g., MuleSoft on-prem v3] | [e.g., migrated to cloud iPaaS + event bus hybrid] |

### 30.2 Roadmap

```mermaid
gantt
    title Integration Architecture Roadmap
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD

    section Phase 1 - Foundations
    API gateway deployment              :a1, 2025-01-01, 45d

```

### 30.3 Deprecated & Decommission Plan

| Component | Deprecation Date | Replacement | Migration Plan |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Legacy ESB — MuleSoft on-prem v3] | YYYY-MM-DD | Cloud iPaaS + Kafka | [Link to migration plan] |
| [API v1 — Customer API] | YYYY-MM-DD | API v2 | [Link to migration guide] |
| [Direct DB integration with legacy CRM] | YYYY-MM-DD | CRM REST API | [Link] |
| [Component] | [Date] | [Replacement] | [Plan link] |

---

## 31. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| ACL | Anti-Corruption Layer — adapter pattern insulating internal domain from external models |
| ADR | Architecture Decision Record |
| API | Application Programming Interface |
| AS2 | Applicability Statement 2 — protocol for B2B EDI message exchange over HTTPS |
| AsyncAPI | Specification standard for event-driven / async APIs |
| B2B | Business-to-Business integration |
| BFF | Backend for Frontend — API layer tailored to specific client types |
| CDC | Change Data Capture |
| Circuit Breaker | Resilience pattern that stops calls to a failing downstream after a threshold is reached |
| CloudEvents | CNCF specification for describing event data in a common format |
| Contract Test | Test that verifies a consumer's expectations against a producer's API |
| CQRS | Command Query Responsibility Segregation |
| Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) | Destination for messages that could not be processed after max retry attempts |
| EDI | Electronic Data Interchange — standard format for B2B document exchange |
| ESB | Enterprise Service Bus — middleware for routing and transforming messages |
| Event Sourcing | Pattern where state changes are stored as an ordered sequence of events |
| Idempotency | Property where an operation produces the same result when applied multiple times |
| iPaaS | Integration Platform as a Service — cloud-based integration middleware |
| JWT | JSON Web Token — compact, URL-safe token for transmitting claims |
| Kafka | Apache Kafka — distributed event streaming platform |
| mTLS | Mutual TLS — both client and server authenticate each other via certificates |
| OAuth 2.0 | Authorization framework for delegated access |
| OIDC | OpenID Connect — identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 |
| OpenAPI | Specification standard for REST APIs (formerly Swagger) |
| Pact | Consumer-driven contract testing framework |
| Pub/Sub | Publish/Subscribe messaging pattern |
| Retry with Backoff | Pattern of retrying a failed call with increasing delay between attempts |
| RTO | Recovery Time Objective |
| RPO | Recovery Point Objective |
| Saga | Pattern for managing distributed transactions via compensating actions |
| Schema Registry | Central store for versioned message/event schemas |
| Service Mesh | Infrastructure layer managing service-to-service communication (mTLS, observability, traffic) |
| SFTP | SSH File Transfer Protocol |
| SPIFFE | Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone — workload identity standard |
| Strangler Fig | Pattern for incrementally replacing a legacy system by routing traffic to new components |
| Webhook | HTTP callback — server pushes event to a registered URL when something happens |
| X.509 | Standard for public key certificates used in TLS and mTLS |
| [Term] | [Definition] |

---

content:
# Solution Architecture Document: [Solution / Project Name]

28. [Glossary](#28-glossary)
29. [References & Appendix](#29-references--appendix)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

> A concise 3-5 paragraph summary written for a non-technical executive audience. No jargon. Focus on the problem being solved, the proposed solution, key trade-offs, and expected outcomes.

[Organization Name] is facing [brief description of the problem or opportunity]. Left unaddressed, this results in [business impact — e.g., lost revenue, operational risk, poor customer experience].

This document describes the recommended solution architecture for [Solution Name], which [high-level description of what the solution does]. The approach has been designed to [key qualities — e.g., minimize time to market, reuse existing investments, ensure regulatory compliance].

**Key outcomes the solution delivers:**

- [Outcome 1 — e.g., reduces order processing time from 4 hours to under 5 minutes]
- [Outcome 2 — e.g., enables [X] business users to self-serve data without engineering support]
- [Outcome 3 — e.g., achieves 99.9% availability SLA with automated failover]
- [Outcome 4 — e.g., delivers full GDPR compliance by [date]]

**Estimated investment:** $[X] build + $[X]/month operating cost
**Target go-live:** [Q / YYYY]
**Key risk:** [One-line summary of the top risk and its mitigation]

---

## 2. Introduction

### 2.1 Purpose of This Document

This Solution Architecture Document (SAD) defines the architecture for [Solution Name]. It captures the problem, proposed solution design, key decisions, trade-offs, risks, and implementation guidance. It is the authoritative reference for all technical and delivery decisions on this initiative.

### 2.2 Scope

| In Scope | Out of Scope |
|---|---|
| [Component / capability 1] | [Explicitly excluded item 1] |
| [Component / capability 2] | [Explicitly excluded item 2] |
| [Deployment environments: dev, staging, prod] | [Adjacent system not being changed] |
| [Integration with systems X, Y, Z] | [Future phase features] |
| [Data migration from legacy system] | [Business process redesign] |

### 2.3 Intended Audience

| Audience | Sections of Interest |
|---|---|
| Executive Sponsor / Steering Committee | 1, 3, 4, 8, 21, 27 |
| Solution / Enterprise Architect | All |
| Development Team Lead | 9, 10, 11, 12, 20 |
| Infrastructure / Platform Engineer | 13, 17, 18, 19 |
| Security Engineer | 14, 16, 22 |
| Data Architect | 11 |
| QA Lead | 7, 20 |
| Project / Delivery Manager | 3, 25, 26, 27 |
| Operations / SRE | 17, 18, 19 |
| Business Analyst | 3, 7, 15 |

### 2.4 Document Status & Review History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes | Reviewer | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Initial draft | — | Draft |
| 0.2 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Added security section | [Name] | In Review |
| 1.0 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Approved final | [Name] | Approved |

### 2.5 Relationship to Other Documents

| Document | Relationship |
|---|---|
| [Enterprise / Reference Architecture] | Parent — this solution must align with enterprise patterns |
| [Business Requirements Document (BRD)] | Source of business requirements (Section 7) |
| [Integration Architecture Doc] | Integration patterns this solution follows |
| [Data Architecture Doc] | Data platform this solution contributes to / consumes |
| [Security Architecture Doc] | Security controls this solution must implement |
| [Project Plan] | Delivery schedule aligned with Section 27 phasing |

---

## 3. Business Context & Problem Statement

### 3.1 Background

[Provide 2-3 paragraphs of business context. Describe the current state, how the organization got here, and why change is needed now. Be specific enough that a new team member can understand the situation without prior knowledge.]

### 3.2 Problem Statement

> **As [persona / organization], we need [capability], so that [business outcome]. Today, [current pain point / limitation] prevents this.**

[Elaborate on the problem in 1-2 paragraphs. Quantify the impact where possible — e.g., "this manual process costs [X] engineer-hours/week" or "system downtime has caused $[X] in lost revenue over the past year."]

### 3.3 Current State

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph CurrentState["Current State — As-Is Architecture"]
        USER_NOW[User] -->|manual process| STEP1[Step 1\ne.g. Email request]
        STEP1 --> STEP2[Step 2\ne.g. Manual data entry]
        STEP2 --> STEP3[Step 3\ne.g. Spreadsheet aggregation]
        STEP3 --> OUTPUT_NOW[Output\ne.g. Weekly report]
        LEGACY[(Legacy System\ne.g. On-prem DB)]
        STEP2 --> LEGACY
    end
```

**Current state pain points:**

| Pain Point | Impact | Frequency |
|---|---|---|
| [e.g., Manual reconciliation process] | [X hours/week of engineer time] | Weekly |
| [e.g., No real-time visibility into orders] | [Customer complaints; SLA breaches] | Daily |
| [e.g., Data siloed across 3 systems] | [Inconsistent reporting; decisions on stale data] | Ongoing |
| [e.g., Single point of failure on legacy DB] | [2 outages in last 6 months; [X]h MTTR] | Recurring |

### 3.4 Desired Future State

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph FutureState["Future State — To-Be Architecture"]
        USER_NEW[User] -->|self-service portal| PORTAL[Web Portal]
        PORTAL --> API[API Layer]
        API --> SVC[Services]
        SVC --> DB_NEW[(Modern DB)]
        SVC --> EVENT[Event Bus]
        EVENT --> NOTIFY[Notifications]
        EVENT --> ANALYTICS[Analytics]
        OUTPUT_NEW[Real-time dashboard\n+ automated reports]
        ANALYTICS --> OUTPUT_NEW
    end
```

### 3.5 Business Objectives & Success Metrics

| Objective | KPI | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Obj 1 — Reduce manual effort] | Hours/week on [process] | [X]h/week | < [Y]h/week | Time tracking |
| [Obj 2 — Improve availability] | Uptime % | [X]% | > 99.9% | Monitoring |
| [Obj 3 — Faster time to insight] | Report generation time | [X]h | < [Y]min | System metrics |
| [Obj 4 — User adoption] | Active users / week | [X] | [Y] | Analytics |
| [Obj 5] | [KPI] | [Baseline] | [Target] | [Method] |

## 4. Solution Overview

### 4.1 Solution Description

[2-3 paragraphs describing the solution at a conceptual level. What does it do? What key capabilities does it provide? What architectural style has been chosen (e.g., microservices, event-driven, serverless) and why?]

### 4.2 Solution Context Diagram (C4 Level 1)

```mermaid
C4Context
    title Solution Context — [Solution Name]

    Person(enduser, "End User", "Uses the solution via web or mobile")
    Person(admin, "Administrator", "Configures and monitors the solution")
    Person(partner, "External Partner", "Integrates via API or data exchange")

    System(solution, "[Solution Name]", "Provides [primary capability description]")

    System_Ext(idp, "Identity Provider", "Authentication & SSO — Auth0 / Okta / Azure AD")
    System_Ext(crm, "CRM System", "Customer master data — Salesforce")
    System_Ext(erp, "ERP System", "Finance & operations — SAP")
    System_Ext(notify, "Notification Service", "Email / SMS — SendGrid / Twilio")
    System_Ext(legacy, "Legacy System", "Existing [system] to be integrated or replaced")

    Rel(enduser, solution, "Uses", "HTTPS")
    Rel(admin, solution, "Manages", "HTTPS")
    Rel(partner, solution, "Integrates via", "REST API / SFTP")
    Rel(solution, idp, "Authenticates via", "OIDC / SAML")
    Rel(solution, crm, "Syncs customer data", "REST / iPaaS")
    Rel(solution, erp, "Exchanges transactions", "REST / EDI")
    Rel(solution, notify, "Sends notifications via", "REST")
    Rel(solution, legacy, "Reads/writes via", "DB adapter / API")
```

### 4.3 Key Solution Capabilities

| Capability | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| [Cap 1 — e.g., Self-service portal] | [Description] | Must Have |
| [Cap 2 — e.g., Real-time notifications] | [Description] | Must Have |
| [Cap 3 — e.g., Reporting dashboard] | [Description] | Must Have |
| [Cap 4 — e.g., Partner API] | [Description] | Should Have |
| [Cap 5 — e.g., Mobile app] | [Description] | Could Have |

### 4.4 What This Solution Is NOT

> Explicitly stating what is out of scope prevents scope creep and misaligned expectations.

- This solution does **not** replace [system X] — it integrates with it
- This solution does **not** include [capability Y] — that is planned for Phase 2
- This solution does **not** provide [feature Z] — that is owned by [other team/system]

## 5. Architecture Principles & Constraints

### 5.1 Governing Architecture Principles

> These are inherited from the enterprise/reference architecture and must be followed. Any deviation requires an ADR.

| # | Principle | Source | Impact on This Solution |
|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | API-first design | Enterprise Architecture | All capabilities exposed via versioned APIs |
| P2 | Security by default | Security Policy | Auth/authz on every endpoint; no plaintext secrets |
| P3 | Cloud-native preferred | Enterprise Architecture | Managed services over self-hosted where practical |
| P4 | Automate everything | Engineering Standards | IaC for infra; CI/CD for all deployments |
| P5 | Design for failure | Enterprise Architecture | No single points of failure; circuit breakers required |
| P6 | Observability built-in | Operations Standards | Structured logs, metrics, and traces from day one |
| P7 | [Principle] | [Source] | [Impact] |

### 5.2 Solution-Specific Principles

| # | Principle | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| SP1 | [e.g., Event-driven where latency allows] | [Reason specific to this solution] |
| SP2 | [e.g., Reuse existing platform services] | [Reason — e.g., avoid duplication, leverage existing SLAs] |
| SP3 | [e.g., Mobile-first UX] | [Reason — e.g., majority of users are field workers] |

### 5.3 Constraints

| Type | Constraint | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| **Technology** | [e.g., Must integrate with SAP ECC 6.0] | [Limited to supported integration methods] |
| **Technology** | [e.g., Must deploy on AWS; no GCP/Azure] | [AWS service choices only] |
| **Budget** | [e.g., Build budget capped at $[X]] | [MVP scope; no custom ML features] |
| **Budget** | [e.g., Opex cap of $[X]/month] | [Managed services only; no dedicated infra] |
| **Regulatory** | [e.g., All PII must remain in EU] | [EU region deployment; no cross-region PII replication] |
| **Regulatory** | [e.g., HIPAA compliance required] | [PHI encryption, audit logging, BAAs with vendors] |
| **Timeline** | [e.g., MVP must go live by Q2 YYYY] | [Phase 1 scope limited; no complex features] |
| **Org / People** | [e.g., Team of [X] engineers available] | [Architecture must be operable by a small team] |
| **Org / People** | [e.g., No new technology stack approvals in flight] | [Must use approved tech stack list] |

### 5.4 Assumptions

| # | Assumption | Owner | Risk if Wrong |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | [e.g., Legacy system API will be available for integration] | [Legacy team] | [Requires different integration approach; delays] |
| A2 | [e.g., Cloud budget will be approved by [date]] | [Finance] | [Delays infrastructure provisioning] |
| A3 | [e.g., Existing Identity Provider (Auth0) can be reused] | [Platform team] | [Must provision new IdP; adds [X] weeks] |
| A4 | [e.g., Data volume will not exceed [X] GB/day in year 1] | [Product] | [Scalability architecture may need revision] |
| A5 | [Assumption] | [Owner] | [Risk if wrong] |

---

## 6. Stakeholders & Concerns

### 6.1 Stakeholder Register

| Stakeholder | Role | Interest | Influence | Key Concerns |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Name / Title] | Executive Sponsor | High | High | ROI, delivery date, risk |
| [Name / Title] | Product Owner | High | High | Feature completeness, UX |
| [Name / Title] | Solution Architect | High | High | Design quality, tech debt |
| [Name / Title] | Engineering Lead | High | Medium | Buildability, team capacity |
| [Name / Title] | Security Lead | Medium | High | Data protection, compliance |
| [Name / Title] | Operations Lead | Medium | Medium | Operability, on-call burden |

### 9.2 Logical Layer Breakdown

| Layer | Components | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| **Presentation** | Web Portal, Admin Console, Mobile App | User interaction; renders data; no business logic |
| **Gateway** | API Gateway, WAF, CDN | Authentication, rate limiting, routing, TLS termination |
| **Aggregation** | BFF (Web), BFF (Mobile), GraphQL Gateway | Client-specific aggregation and response shaping |
| **Application** | Service A, Service B, Service C | Domain business logic; each owns its data store |
| **Messaging** | Event Broker, Background Workers | Async workflows, decoupling, event-driven processing |
| **Data** | PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, S3 | Persistence, caching, object storage |
| **Platform** | Auth, Config, Secrets, CI/CD | Cross-cutting concerns shared across all services |

### 10.3 API Contract Standards

- **External APIs:** OpenAPI 3.x spec; published to developer portal

- **Event APIs:** AsyncAPI spec; schemas in Schema Registry
- **Versioning:** URI path versioning (`/v1/`, `/v2/`) for REST; package versioning for Protobuf
- **Error format:** RFC 7807 Problem Details for all REST errors

```mermaid
flowchart LR
```

### 11.2 Data Store Design

| Store | Technology | Purpose | Schema Owner | Scaling Plan |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Service A DB] | PostgreSQL [version] | [Domain A] transactional data | [Service A team] | Read replicas; connection pooling (PgBouncer) |
| [Service B DB] | MongoDB [version] | [Domain B] flexible document data | [Service B team] | Atlas auto-sharding |
| [Cache] | Redis [version] | Session store, hot-path cache, rate limit counters | Platform | Redis Cluster mode |

| [Order] | [Order Service DB] | DWH, Fulfilment Service | `order_id` (UUID) | Immutable after [X]h |
| [Product] | [Product Service DB] | Search index, DWH | `product_id` + `sku` | Versioned; SCD Type 2 |
| [User Profile] | [Profile Service DB] | Auth service, DWH | `user_id` (UUID) | PII — masked in DWH |
| [Entity] | [Service] | [Targets] | [Key] | [Notes] |

### 11.4 Data Governance Summary

- **Data classification:** Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted (PII)
- **PII fields:** [email, name, phone, IP] — masked in non-prod; tokenized in event streams
- **Retention:** Operational data [X] years; audit logs [X] years; analytics [X] years
- **GDPR right to erasure:** Propagated via `user.deletion_requested` event to all owning services
- **Data lineage:** Tracked via [OpenLineage / DataHub] for analytical datasets

## 14. Security Architecture

### 14.1 Threat Model Summary

> Full threat model: [Link to STRIDE/PASTA threat model document]

| Threat | Attack Vector | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Account takeover | Stolen credentials / session | Medium | High | MFA; short-lived tokens; anomaly detection |
| API abuse / scraping | Unauthenticated or over-privileged API | Medium | Medium | Rate limiting; RBAC; API key scoping |
| Injection (SQLi, XSS) | Malformed user input | Medium | High | Input validation; parameterized queries; CSP headers |
| Data exfiltration | Compromised service account | Low | Critical | Least privilege; egress controls; DLP |
| SSRF | User-controlled URL in API | Low | High | Allowlist outbound destinations; block internal ranges |
| Dependency vulnerability | Unpatched library | High | Medium | Automated SCA (Snyk / Dependabot); patch SLA |
| [Threat] | [Vector] | [L/M/H] | [L/M/H] | [Mitigation] |

### 14.2 Security Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    INET[Internet] --> WAF_SEC[WAF\nOWASP rules, DDoS protection]
    WAF_SEC --> CDN_SEC[CDN / TLS Termination\nTLS 1.3 only]
    CDN_SEC --> GW_SEC[API Gateway\nJWT validation, rate limiting, IP filtering]
    GW_SEC --> MESH_SEC[Service Mesh\nmTLS between all internal services]
    MESH_SEC --> SVC_SEC[Services\nInput validation, RBAC, audit logging]
    SVC_SEC --> DB_SEC[(Databases\nEncryption at rest AES-256\nLeast-privilege service accounts)]
    SVC_SEC --> SECRETS_SEC[Secrets Manager\nVault / AWS Secrets Manager]
```

### 14.3 Security Controls Checklist

| Control | Required | Implementation | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| TLS 1.2+ on all endpoints | Must Have | TLS termination at CDN/ALB | [Done / Planned] |
| OAuth 2.0 + OIDC authentication | Must Have | Auth0 / Okta | [Done / Planned] |
| JWT short-lived tokens (< 15 min) | Must Have | Auth service config | [Done / Planned] |
| RBAC on all APIs | Must Have | API Gateway + service layer | [Done / Planned] |
| Secrets in Secrets Manager | Must Have | HashiCorp Vault / AWS SM | [Done / Planned] |
| No secrets in source code / images | Must Have | Pre-commit + CI secret scan | [Done / Planned] |
| Input validation on all inputs | Must Have | Framework middleware | [Done / Planned] |
| Container image scanning | Must Have | Trivy / Grype in CI | [Done / Planned] |
| Dependency vulnerability scanning | Must Have | Snyk / Dependabot | [Done / Planned] |
| SAST in CI pipeline | Must Have | Semgrep / SonarQube | [Done / Planned] |
| Penetration test before go-live | Must Have | [External vendor] | [Planned — date] |
| Encryption at rest (AES-256) | Must Have | DB + S3 native encryption | [Done / Planned] |
| PII masked in logs | Must Have | Structured logging middleware | [Done / Planned] |
| Audit logging (who/what/when) | Must Have | Audit service / log sink | [Done / Planned] |
| WAF rules tuned | Should Have | Managed rule sets + custom | [Done / Planned] |
| mTLS internal service communication | Should Have | Istio STRICT mode | [Done / Planned] |

## 16. Identity & Access Management

### 16.1 Authentication Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant APP as Web App
    participant GW as API Gateway
    participant IDP as Identity Provider (Auth0/Okta)
    participant SVC as Backend Service

    Note over U,SVC: Authorization Code + PKCE (User Login)
    U->>APP: Navigate to app
    APP->>IDP: Redirect to /authorize (PKCE challenge)
    IDP-->>U: Login prompt
    U->>IDP: Enter credentials + MFA
    IDP-->>APP: Auth code
    APP->>IDP: Exchange code for tokens
    IDP-->>APP: access_token (JWT 15min) + refresh_token (7d)
    APP->>GW: API request with Bearer token
    GW->>IDP: Validate JWT (JWKS)
    GW->>SVC: Forward + inject user claims
    SVC-->>APP: Response
```

### 16.2 Authorization Model

- **Model:** RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) with ABAC extensions for fine-grained rules
- **Roles definition:**

| Role | Description | Permissions |
|---|---|---|
| `admin` | Full system administrator | All operations across all resources |
| `manager` | Department / team manager | Read/write own team's resources; read others |
| `user` | Standard end user | Read/write own resources only |
| `readonly` | Reporting / audit access | Read-only on permitted resources |
| `service` | Machine-to-machine | Scoped to specific service operations |
| `[role]` | [Description] | [Permissions] |

### 16.3 SSO & Federation

| Scenario | Method | IdP | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Employee login | SAML 2.0 / OIDC | [Okta / Azure AD] | Corporate directory federated |
| External user login | OAuth 2.0 + OIDC | [Auth0] | Email/password + social login |
| Partner access | OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials | [Auth0] | API key + scoped token |
| Machine-to-machine | Client Credentials | [Auth0 / internal] | Per-service client ID |

## 17. Resilience & Disaster Recovery

### 17.1 Resilience Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    REQ[Inbound Request]

    REQ --> LB_R[Load Balancer\nMulti-AZ routing]
    LB_R --> CB[Circuit Breaker\nTrip on [X]% errors in [X]s]
    CB -- closed --> SVC_R[Service\nStateless; auto-restart on crash]
    CB -- open --> FALLBACK_R[Fallback\nCached / degraded response]
    SVC_R --> RETRY_R{Retry?\n< [X] attempts}
    RETRY_R -- yes --> BACK_R[Exponential Backoff\n+ Jitter]
    BACK_R --> SVC_R
    RETRY_R -- no --> DLQ_R[DLQ / Error\nResponse]
```

### 17.2 Failure Mode Analysis

| Failure Scenario | Components Affected | Detection | Mitigation | Recovery |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single AZ outage | [X]% of pods + one DB replica | LB health checks | Multi-AZ deployment; auto-failover | Automatic — < [X]min |
| DB primary failure | All write operations | Health check + monitoring alert | Automated failover to replica | < [X]min via RDS/managed DB |
| Upstream API down (e.g., Stripe) | Payment feature | Circuit breaker + health check | Circuit open; queue retry; graceful degradation | Automatic on recovery |
| Kafka broker failure | All async workflows | Kafka metrics; consumer lag spike | Replication factor 3; MirrorMaker2 for DR | Auto-leader election; < [X]min |
| Memory leak / pod crash | Single service | Liveness probe; OOMKilled event | K8s auto-restart; HPA scales out | < [X]min auto-restart |
| Full region outage | All services | External synthetic monitoring | DR region warm standby | RTO < [X]min — manual failover |
| DDoS attack | API gateway, CDN | WAF anomaly detection; traffic spike alert | WAF rules; rate limiting; CDN absorbs | Automatic |
| Data corruption | DB / storage | Data quality checks; reconciliation alerts | PITR backups; immutable backups | Restore within RPO = [X]min |

### 17.3 Recovery Objectives

| Scenario | RTO | RPO | Recovery Method | Tested |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single service crash | < 2 min | 0 | K8s auto-restart | Automated (liveness probes) |
| DB primary failure | < [X] min | < [X] min | Automated DB failover | [Quarterly] |
| AZ outage | < [X] min | 0 | Multi-AZ LB re-routes | [Monthly chaos test] |
| Full region outage | < [X] min | < [X] min | DR region failover | [Bi-annually] |
| Data corruption | < [X] min restore | < [X] min data loss | PITR restore | [Monthly restore test] |

### 17.4 Backup Strategy

| Asset | Backup Type | Frequency | Retention | Restore Test |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Primary DB | Continuous WAL + daily snapshot | Continuous + daily | [X] days | Monthly |
| MongoDB | Oplog-based continuous | Continuous + daily | [X] days | Monthly |
| Object Store (S3) | Cross-region replication | Continuous | [X] days | Quarterly |
| Kafka topics | MirrorMaker2 to DR region | Continuous | [X] days | Quarterly |
| Application config | IaC in Git | On change | Indefinite | On change |

## 18. Scalability & Performance

### 18.1 Scaling Strategy

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    LOAD[Incoming Load] --> GW_SCALE[API Gateway\nManaged auto-scale]
    GW_SCALE --> HPA_SCALE[Kubernetes HPA\nScale pods on CPU / custom metrics]
    HPA_SCALE --> POOL_SCALE[Connection Pool\nPgBouncer / DB proxy]
    POOL_SCALE --> DB_SCALE[Database\nVertical + Read Replicas\n→ Aurora Serverless v2]
    HPA_SCALE --> CACHE_SCALE[Cache\nRedis Cluster\nauto-shard]
    HPA_SCALE --> KAFKA_SCALE[Kafka\nAdd partitions + consumer replicas]
```

### 18.2 Scaling Targets

| Component | Min Replicas | Max Replicas | Scale Trigger | Time to Scale |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway | Managed | Managed | Automatic | < 1 min |
| [Service A] | [2] | [20] | CPU > [70]% or RPS > [X] | < 2 min |
| [Service B] | [2] | [10] | CPU > [70]% | < 2 min |
| [Worker] | [2] | [50] | Kafka lag > [X]K messages | < 2 min |
| DB (read replicas) | [1] | [5] | Manual / scheduled | < 5 min |
| Cache (Redis) | Cluster ([X] nodes) | [Y] nodes | Manual | < 10 min |

### 18.3 Performance Benchmarks & Targets

| Scenario | Metric | Target | Test Method |
|---|---|---|---|
| Steady state [X] concurrent users | p99 API latency | < [X]ms | Load test (k6) |
| Peak [X]x normal load | No errors; p99 < [Y]ms | — | Spike test (k6) |
| Database failover | Time to first successful write | < [X]s | Chaos test |
| Cache miss rate | % of requests hitting DB | < [X]% | Cache hit ratio monitoring |
| Cold start (scale from 0) | Time to first successful response | < [X]s | Integration test |
| Bulk import ([X]K records) | Processing time | < [X]min | Integration test |

### 18.4 Caching Strategy

| Layer | Technology | What is Cached | TTL | Invalidation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CDN | [CloudFront / Cloudflare] | Static assets, public API responses | 1yr (assets); [X]min (API) | Cache-control headers; deploy purge |
| API Gateway | [Kong response cache] | Heavily read, slowly changing GET responses | [X]s | Manual purge on write |
| Application (Redis) | Redis | Session tokens, user profiles, rate limit counters | [X]min | Write-through + TTL |
| DB query result | Redis | Expensive aggregation queries | [X]min | TTL expiry |

| Service / Feature | SLI | SLO | SLA |

### 19.3 Alerting Runbook Index

| Alert | Severity | Condition | Runbook |
|---|---|---|---|
| API error rate spike | P1 | 5xx rate > [X]% for [X]min | [Link] |
| Service pod crash-looping | P1 | Restart count > [X] in [X]min | [Link] |
| DB CPU / connections high | P1 | DB CPU > [X]% or connections > [X]% max | [Link] |

### 19.4 Logging Standards

- **Format:** Structured JSON; all logs must include: `timestamp` (ISO-8601 UTC), `level`, `service`, `trace_id`, `span_id`, `message`
- **PII:** Never log PII in plaintext; mask or exclude before logging
- **Log levels:** ERROR (actionable failures), WARN (degraded state), INFO (key business events), DEBUG (dev only — disabled in prod)
- **Retention:** [X] days hot; [X] months warm; [X] years cold archive
- **Correlation:** `trace_id` injected at API gateway; propagated in all downstream calls and events

## 20. Developer Experience & CI/CD

### 20.1 Local Development Setup

```bash
# Clone repository
git clone [repo-url]
cd [project-name]

# Copy environment config
cp .env.example .env.local
# Edit .env.local with local values (never commit this file)

# Start all local dependencies (DB, Kafka, Redis, mock services)
docker compose up -d

# Run database migrations
make db-migrate

# Start all services in development mode (hot reload)
make dev

# Run all tests
make test

# Run linter and formatter
make lint
```

```
[project-root]/
├── services/
```

### 20.3 CI/CD Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    DEV_CD[Developer\npush to branch] --> PR_CD[Pull Request\nCode review]
    PR_CD --> CI_CD

    subgraph CI_CD["CI Pipeline (on every PR)"]
        LINT_CD[Lint & Format check]
        TEST_CD[Unit + Integration Tests]
        CONTRACT_CD[Contract Tests\nPact verification]
        SCAN_CD[Security Scan\nSAST + SCA + secret detection]

    end

    CI_CD --> STG_CD[Deploy to Staging\nHelmfile / ArgoCD]

    E2E_CD --> APPROVE_CD{Manual Approval\nor auto for non-breaking}
    APPROVE_CD --> PROD_CD[Deploy to Production\nCanary → [X]% → 100%]
    PROD_CD --> MONITOR_CD[Post-deploy monitoring\n15-min observation window]
    MONITOR_CD -- regression detected --> ROLLBACK_CD[Automatic Rollback]
```

### 20.4 Deployment Strategy

| Strategy | Used For | Rollback Time | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rolling update | Standard service deploys | < [X]min | Default; zero-downtime |
| Blue/Green | DB schema migrations; high-risk changes | Instant traffic switch | Requires double capacity briefly |
| Canary | New features; uncertain risk level | Instant flag revert | Ramp: 5% → 25% → 100% |
| Feature flags | Dark launches, A/B tests, gradual rollout | Instant toggle | [LaunchDarkly / Unleash / custom] |

### 21.2 Cost Optimization Levers

- **Compute:** Spot/preemptible for non-critical workers and batch; committed use for baseline
- **Database:** Auto-pause non-prod DBs outside business hours; right-size on [quarterly] reviews
- **Storage:** S3 lifecycle policies — Intelligent-Tiering for uncertain access patterns
- **CDN:** Maximize cache hit ratio; reduce origin requests
- **Observability:** Sample high-volume traces; set retention per log severity
- **Auto-scaling:** Scale to zero for non-prod; scale down aggressively after peak

### 22.2 Privacy by Design Checklist

- [ ] Data minimization — only collect data needed for stated purpose
- [ ] PII inventory documented — all PII fields identified and classified
- [ ] PII masked in non-production environments
- [ ] PII excluded from logs and traces
- [ ] Data retention periods defined and enforced via automation
- [ ] Right to erasure propagated across all services holding user data
- [ ] DPA (Data Processing Agreement) signed with all third-party vendors handling PII
- [ ] Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) completed for high-risk processing
- [ ] Consent management implemented where required

### 22.3 Audit & Compliance Logging

- **What is logged:** All authentication events, data access, data mutations, admin actions, API key usage
- **Log fields:** `timestamp`, `actor_id`, `actor_role`, `action`, `resource_type`, `resource_id`, `outcome`, `ip_address`, `trace_id`
- **Storage:** Immutable audit log store — [CloudTrail / dedicated audit DB with append-only policy]
- **Retention:** [X] years (per [regulation requirement])
- **Access:** Audit logs accessible only to [Security, Legal, Compliance] roles

## 23. Migration & Transition Plan

### 23.1 Migration Strategy

> Select and describe the migration approach.

| Strategy | Description | Applicable When |
|---|---|---|
| **Big Bang** | Cutover all at once on a set date | Simple migrations; short downtime window acceptable |
| **Strangler Fig** | Route traffic progressively to new system; shrink old | Large systems; zero-downtime required |
| **Parallel Run** | Both systems run simultaneously; compare outputs | High-risk migrations; validation needed |
| **Phased Migration** | Migrate by domain / module / user cohort | Complex systems; long migration runway |

**Selected strategy:** [Strategy name]
**Rationale:** [Why this strategy was selected for this migration]

### 23.2 Migration Phases

```mermaid
flowchart LR

    PHASE1[Phase 1\nFoundation\nNew system deployed\nShadow mode\nData sync running]
    PHASE2[Phase 2\nPilot\nLimited user cohort\n[X]% traffic\nMonitoring & compare]
    PHASE3[Phase 3\nFull Migration\n100% traffic to new\nLegacy read-only]

    PHASE0 --> PHASE1 --> PHASE2 --> PHASE3 --> PHASE4
```

| Q04 | [e.g., Is multi-tenancy required from day 1 or Phase 2?] | [Arch] | YYYY-MM-DD | YYYY-MM-DD | Resolved | [Single-tenant for MVP; multi-tenant in Phase 2 — ADR-009] |
| Q-NN | [Question] | [Raised By] | [Date] | [Target Date] | [Status] | [Resolution] |

---

## 27. Roadmap & Phasing

### 27.1 Phased Delivery Plan

> Breaking delivery into phases reduces risk, enables early feedback, and delivers value incrementally.

| Phase | Name | Key Deliverables | Timeline | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 0 | Foundation | Cloud infra, CI/CD pipeline, core platform services, dev environment | [X] weeks | Devs can deploy a hello-world service to staging |
| Phase 1 | MVP | Core [capability A], [capability B], basic UI, key integrations | [X] weeks | [X] pilot users can complete end-to-end workflow |
| Phase 2 | Beta | [Capability C], partner API, analytics dashboard, full security hardening | [X] weeks | [X] users; security pen test passed; SLOs met |
| Phase 3 | GA | Full feature set, mobile app, self-service onboarding, legacy decommission | [X] weeks | All NFRs met; legacy migrated; full monitoring live |
| Phase 4 | Optimise | Performance tuning, cost optimisation, advanced analytics, ML features | Ongoing | Cost targets met; NPS > [X] |

### 27.2 Roadmap Gantt

```mermaid
gantt
    title Solution Delivery Roadmap
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD

    section Phase 0 — Foundation
    Cloud infra provisioning           :p0a, 2025-01-01, 14d

    Core platform services             :p0c, 2025-01-14, 14d
    Dev environment + onboarding       :p0d, 2025-01-07, 21d

    section Phase 1 — MVP

    Service B — [domain]               :p1b, 2025-02-01, 35d
    Web portal — core screens          :p1c, 2025-02-07, 35d
    Key integrations (CRM, IdP)        :p1d, 2025-02-14, 28d

    section Phase 2 — Beta

    section Phase 3 — GA
```

### 27.3 MVP Scope Definition

**In MVP (Phase 1):**
- [Feature / capability 1]
- [Feature / capability 2]
- [Feature / capability 3]
- [Minimum required integration: X]

**Deferred to Phase 2+:**
- [Feature Y — deferred reason]
- [Feature Z — deferred reason]
- [Mobile app — deferred to Phase 3]

---

## 28. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| ACL | Anti-Corruption Layer — adapter pattern that insulates a domain from external model pollution |
| ADR | Architecture Decision Record — documents significant architectural choices and their rationale |
| API | Application Programming Interface |
| BFF | Backend for Frontend — API layer tailored to specific client type (web, mobile) |
| CDC | Change Data Capture — captures row-level database changes as a stream |
| CDN | Content Delivery Network |
| CI/CD | Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery |
| Circuit Breaker | Resilience pattern that stops calls to a failing downstream after an error threshold |
| CQRS | Command Query Responsibility Segregation |
| DR | Disaster Recovery |
| EDA | Event-Driven Architecture |
| HPA | Horizontal Pod Autoscaler — Kubernetes mechanism for scaling pods based on metrics |
| IaC | Infrastructure as Code |
| IdP | Identity Provider — system that manages user identities and issues tokens |
| JWT | JSON Web Token |
| KPI | Key Performance Indicator |
| LCP | Largest Contentful Paint — Core Web Vitals page load metric |
| mTLS | Mutual TLS — both client and server authenticate via certificates |
| NFR | Non-Functional Requirement |
| OAuth 2.0 | Authorization framework for delegated access |
| OIDC | OpenID Connect — identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 |
| PII | Personally Identifiable Information |
| PITR | Point-In-Time Recovery |
| RBAC | Role-Based Access Control |
| RFC 7807 | IETF standard for HTTP API error response format (Problem Details) |
| RPO | Recovery Point Objective — maximum acceptable data loss after a failure |
| RTO | Recovery Time Objective — maximum acceptable downtime duration |
| SAD | Solution Architecture Document — this document |
| SLA | Service Level Agreement — contractual uptime / performance commitment |
| SLI | Service Level Indicator — a measurable metric (e.g., error rate) |
| SLO | Service Level Objective — target value for an SLI |
| SPA | Single Page Application |
| SPIFFE | Secure Production Identity Framework — workload identity standard |
| WAF | Web Application Firewall |
| WCAG | Web Content Accessibility Guidelines |
| [Term] | [Definition] |

---

## 29. References & Appendix

### Internal References

### External Standards & Frameworks

- [C4 Model for Architecture Diagrams](https://c4model.com/)
- [AWS Well-Architected Framework](https://aws.amazon.com/architecture/well-architected/)
- [Google Cloud Architecture Framework](https://cloud.google.com/architecture/framework)
- [OWASP API Security Top 10](https://owasp.org/www-project-api-security/)
- [12-Factor App](https://12factor.net/)
- [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/)
- [TOGAF — Architecture Development Method](https://www.opengroup.org/togaf)
- [ISO/IEC 25010 — Software Quality Model](https://iso25000.com/index.php/en/iso-25000-standards/iso-25010)

### Appendix A: Architecture Review Checklist

> Use this checklist during architecture review sessions.

**Functional correctness:**
- [ ] All functional requirements (Section 7.1) addressed in the design
- [ ] All NFRs (Section 7.2) have a corresponding architectural mechanism

**Architecture quality:**
- [ ] No single points of failure identified
- [ ] All external dependencies have circuit breakers or fallback behaviour
- [ ] Data ownership is clear for every entity
- [ ] All APIs versioned; breaking change process defined

**Security:**
- [ ] Threat model reviewed and mitigations in place
- [ ] Authentication and authorization on every endpoint
- [ ] PII fields identified; handling defined (masking, retention, deletion)
- [ ] Secrets management approach defined; no secrets in code

**Operability:**
- [ ] SLOs defined for all user-facing features
- [ ] Alerting configured with runbooks for all P1/P2 scenarios
- [ ] RTO/RPO defined; recovery mechanisms designed and tested
- [ ] Deployment strategy supports zero-downtime deploys

**Cost:**
- [ ] Cost estimate reviewed and within budget
- [ ] Auto-scaling bounds prevent runaway spend
- [ ] Non-prod environments shut down outside business hours

**Compliance:**
- [ ] Applicable regulations identified; requirements mapped to design
- [ ] DPA signed with all third-party vendors processing PII
- [ ] Audit logging in place for required actions

### Appendix B: Decision Log (Minor Decisions)

> For decisions too small for a full ADR but worth recording.

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| YYYY-MM-DD | [e.g., Use Snappy compression for Kafka messages] | [Better compression ratio vs CPU trade-off for our message size] | [Arch] |
| YYYY-MM-DD | [e.g., Set JWT expiry to 15 minutes] | [Security team guidance; refresh token handles UX] | [Security] |
| YYYY-MM-DD | [Decision] | [Rationale] | [Owner] |

# Technical Architecture Document: [System / Platform Name]

> **Document Version:** 1.0

> **Owner:** [Technical Architect / Engineering Lead]

> **Technology Domain:** [e.g., Backend Platform / Data Platform / Mobile / Full-Stack]

---

34. [Glossary](#34-glossary)
35. [References](#35-references)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

> A concise technical summary for engineering leadership and senior engineers. More detailed than a business executive summary but still high-level enough for someone not deeply familiar with the codebase.

[System Name] is a [brief technical description — e.g., distributed, event-driven microservices platform] designed to [primary technical purpose]. This document describes every layer of the technical architecture: from the runtime environment and language choices down to caching strategies, connection pool sizing, and deployment pipelines.

**Core technical characteristics:**

- **Architecture style:** [e.g., microservices / modular monolith / serverless / event-driven]
- **Primary languages:** [e.g., Go 1.22, TypeScript 5.x, Python 3.12]
- **Deployment target:** [e.g., Kubernetes on AWS EKS, multi-AZ, eu-west-1 primary]
- **Data stores:** [e.g., PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, S3, Kafka]
- **Availability target:** [e.g., 99.9% — less than 8.7h downtime/year]
- **Performance target:** [e.g., p99 API latency < 200ms at 5,000 RPS]

**Key technical decisions:**

- [Decision 1 — e.g., gRPC for all internal service communication]
- [Decision 2 — e.g., event-driven async for order workflow using Kafka]
- [Decision 3 — e.g., Postgres with row-level security for multi-tenancy]
- [Decision 4 — e.g., OpenTelemetry for vendor-neutral observability]

---

## 2. Introduction

### 2.1 Purpose

This Technical Architecture Document (TAD) is the definitive technical reference for [System Name]. It describes every significant technical decision, component, pattern, and standard used in the system. It is intended to guide implementation, onboard engineers, and serve as the baseline for technical reviews.

### 2.2 Scope

| In Scope | Out of Scope |
|---|---|
| All backend services and their internals | Business / product requirements (see SAD) |
| Data store design, schemas, and access patterns | UI/UX wireframes and design specs |
| Infrastructure, networking, and deployment | Vendor contract details |
| Security controls and their implementation | Project management and delivery tracking |
| Observability stack and alerting | ML model design and training details |
| CI/CD pipelines and developer tooling | [Any other explicit exclusion] |

### 2.3 Intended Audience

| Audience | Primary Sections |
|---|---|
| Engineering Leadership / CTO | 1, 3, 6, 23, 29, 33 |
| Technical Architects | All |
| Senior / Staff Engineers | All |
| Backend Engineers | 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 15 |
| Platform / Infrastructure Engineers | 16, 17, 22, 23, 27, 28 |
| Security Engineers | 18, 19, 20, 24 |
| SRE / Operations | 21, 22, 28 |
| QA / Test Engineers | 26, 27 |
| New Team Members | All (use as onboarding reference) |

### 2.4 Document History

| Version | Date | Author | Summary of Changes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Initial draft |
| 0.2 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Added caching and search sections |
| 1.0 | YYYY-MM-DD | [Name] | Approved — baseline for [milestone] |

---

## 3. System Context & Goals

### 3.1 System Description

[2-3 paragraphs describing the system from a technical viewpoint. What problem does it solve technically? What are its primary runtime characteristics — latency-sensitive, throughput-heavy, compute-intensive, storage-heavy? Who calls it and how?]

### 3.2 System Context Diagram

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context — [System Name]

    Person(devuser, "Developer / API Consumer", "Builds on top of the platform via APIs")
    Person(enduser, "End User", "Uses product via web or mobile")
    Person(opsuser, "Operator / SRE", "Monitors and operates the system")

    System(system, "[System Name]", "Provides [core technical capability]")

    System_Ext(idp, "Identity Provider", "Auth0 / Okta — AuthN + JWT issuance")
    System_Ext(cloud, "Cloud Provider", "AWS / GCP / Azure — managed services")
    System_Ext(observability, "Observability Platform", "Datadog / Grafana Cloud")
    System_Ext(cicd, "CI/CD Platform", "GitHub Actions / CircleCI / ArgoCD")
    System_Ext(saasdep, "SaaS Dependencies", "Stripe, SendGrid, etc.")

    Rel(enduser, system, "Uses via", "HTTPS")
    Rel(devuser, system, "Calls via", "REST / gRPC")
    Rel(opsuser, system, "Monitors via", "Observability tools")
    Rel(system, idp, "Token validation", "HTTPS / JWKS")
    Rel(system, cloud, "Runs on / uses managed services")
    Rel(system, observability, "Emits telemetry")
    Rel(cicd, system, "Deploys to")
    Rel(system, saasdep, "Integrates with", "HTTPS")
```

### 3.3 Technical Goals & Quality Attributes

| Quality Attribute | Goal | Target Metric |
|---|---|---|
| **Performance** | Low-latency user-facing APIs | p50 < [X]ms, p99 < [Y]ms at [Z] RPS |
| **Throughput** | High request volume | [X] RPS sustained; [Y] RPS peak |
| **Availability** | High uptime | [99.9% / 99.99%] — [X]h downtime/year |
| **Scalability** | Horizontal scale on demand | Scale from [X] to [Y] pods within [Z]min |
| **Resilience** | Tolerate component failures | No cascading failures; circuit breaking |
| **Maintainability** | Fast feature velocity | Deploy multiple times per day; < [X]min pipeline |
| **Testability** | High confidence deploys | > [X]% coverage; contract tests on all APIs |
| **Observability** | Rapid fault detection | MTTD < [X]min on P1; full trace on any request |
| **Security** | Protect data and systems | Pass annual pen test; zero critical CVEs in prod |
| **Operability** | Low operational burden | Runbook for every alert; < [X]min MTTR on P1 |

### 3.4 System Constraints

| Constraint | Detail |
|---|---|
| Cloud provider | [AWS / GCP / Azure / Multi-cloud] only |
| Region | Primary: [region]; DR: [region] |
| Approved languages | [Go, TypeScript, Python — others require TAD amendment] |
| Runtime | Kubernetes [version] on [EKS / GKE / AKS] |
| Compliance | [GDPR, SOC 2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA — as applicable] |
| Max p99 latency (external API) | [X]ms — SLA-backed |
| Max service binary size | [X]MB (cold start impact) |
| Approved open-source licenses | MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD — GPL requires approval |

---

## 4. Technical Principles & Standards

### 4.1 Core Technical Principles

| # | Principle | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | **Explicit over implicit** | Config, dependencies, and behavior must be explicit and observable. No magic. |
| T2 | **Fail fast, fail loudly** | Validate inputs at boundaries; panic/crash on unrecoverable errors; never silently swallow errors. |
| T3 | **Immutable infrastructure** | Never mutate running instances; replace via redeploy. Servers are cattle, not pets. |
| T4 | **Twelve-factor app compliance** | Config in environment; stateless processes; explicit dependency declaration. |
| T5 | **Idempotent operations** | All write operations (HTTP POST/PATCH, message processing) must be safe to repeat. |
| T6 | **Schema-first development** | Define data contracts (OpenAPI, Protobuf, JSON Schema, SQL migrations) before implementation. |
| T7 | **Least-privilege by default** | Service accounts, IAM roles, and DB users have only the permissions they need — nothing more. |
| T8 | **No synchronous calls in critical paths where async suffices** | Prefer async to decouple latency, reliability, and scalability. |
| T9 | **Test at the right level** | Unit test logic; integration test boundaries; contract test interfaces; E2E test user journeys. |
| T10 | **Measure before optimising** | All performance work starts with a profiled baseline; no premature optimisation. |

### 4.2 Coding Standards

| Category | Standard | Enforcement |
|---|---|---|
| Language versions | [Go 1.22+, Node.js 20 LTS, Python 3.12+] | CI version pinning |
| Code formatting | [gofmt / Prettier / Black] — zero tolerance for unformatted code | Pre-commit hook + CI |
| Linting | [golangci-lint / ESLint / Ruff] — no warnings in CI | CI gate |
| Cyclomatic complexity | Max [15] per function | Linter rule |
| Function / method length | Max [50] lines (guide); max [80] lines (hard) | Linter rule |
| Test file coverage gate | Minimum [80]% line coverage; [90]% on critical paths | CI gate |
| Dependency updates | Dependabot / Renovate — auto-merge patch; manual review minor/major | Automated PRs |
| Commit message format | [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) | Commitlint in CI |
| Branch naming | `feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/` prefixes | Branch protection rules |

### 4.3 Documentation Standards

- Every public function/method/type has a doc comment
- Every service has a `README.md` with: purpose, local setup, environment variables, API overview
- Every ADR is written within 5 days of the decision being made
- Architecture diagrams updated within one sprint of significant structural change
- Runbooks exist for every production alert

---

## 5. System Architecture Overview

### 5.1 High-Level Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Clients["Clients"]
        WEB[Web App\nReact / Vue]
        MOB[Mobile App\niOS / Android]
        EXT[External Consumers\nPartners / 3rd party]
    end

    subgraph Edge["Edge Layer"]
        CDN[CDN\nCloudFront / Cloudflare]
        WAF[WAF\nOWASP rules]
        GW[API Gateway\nKong / AWS APIGW]
    end

    subgraph Services["Application Services (Kubernetes)"]
        BFF[BFF / Aggregation Layer]
        SVC_A[Service A\nGo]
        SVC_B[Service B\nNode.js]
        SVC_C[Service C\nPython]
        WORKER[Worker\nAsync processors]
    end

    subgraph Messaging["Messaging"]
        KAFKA[Kafka\nEvent bus]
    end

    subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
        PG[(PostgreSQL\nPrimary OLTP)]
        MONGO[(MongoDB\nDocument store)]
        REDIS[(Redis\nCache + sessions)]
        ES[(Elasticsearch\nSearch)]
        S3[(S3 / GCS\nObject store)]
    end

    subgraph Platform["Platform Layer"]
        AUTH[Identity Provider\nAuth0 / Okta]
        VAULT[Secrets\nVault / AWS SM]
        CONFIG[Config\nEnv vars / ConfigMap]
        OTEL[Observability\nOTEL + Grafana]
    end

    Clients --> Edge
    Edge --> Services
    Services <--> Messaging
    Services --> Data
    Services --> Platform
    Messaging --> WORKER
    WORKER --> Data
```

### 5.2 Architecture Style Decision

| Style | Adopted? | Scope | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Microservices | Yes | Core domain services | Independent deployability, team autonomy, targeted scaling |
| Modular monolith | Yes (partial) | [e.g., Admin service — low change rate] | Simpler ops for stable, low-traffic component |
| Event-driven | Yes | Async workflows | Decoupling; resilience; replay capability |
| Serverless | Yes (partial) | [e.g., file processing, scheduled tasks] | No idle cost; auto-scaling to zero |
| CQRS | Yes (partial) | [e.g., Orders domain] | Read/write path separation for performance |
| Event sourcing | No / Yes | [e.g., Audit log only] | [Rationale] |

### 5.3 Service Interaction Patterns

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Sync["Synchronous (gRPC / REST)"]
        S1[Service A] -->|gRPC call\nwhen immediate response needed| S2[Service B]
    end

    subgraph Async["Asynchronous (Kafka)"]
        S3[Service C] -->|publishes event| TOPIC[Kafka Topic]
        TOPIC -->|consumer group A| S4[Service D]
        TOPIC -->|consumer group B| S5[Service E]
    end

    subgraph Query["Query Separation (CQRS)"]
        CMD[Command\nwrite side] --> CMDDB[(Write DB)]
        CMDDB -->|CDC / event| READDB[(Read Model)]
        QRY[Query\nread side] --> READDB
    end
```

---

## 6. Technology Stack

### 6.1 Approved Technology Stack

| Category | Technology | Version | License | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Backend — primary** | [Go] | [1.22+] | BSD | Performance, low memory, fast compile, excellent concurrency |
| **Backend — secondary** | [Node.js] | [20 LTS] | MIT | Team expertise; JavaScript ecosystem for [specific service] |
| **Backend — data/ML** | [Python] | [3.12+] | PSF | Data processing libraries; ML ecosystem |
| **Frontend** | [React] | [18.x] | MIT | Ecosystem size; team expertise; component reuse |
| **Mobile** | [React Native / Flutter] | [version] | MIT | Cross-platform; code sharing |
| **IaC** | [Terraform] | [1.7+] | MPL-2.0 | Declarative; mature; large provider ecosystem |
| **Container runtime** | Docker | [26+] | Apache 2.0 | Industry standard |
| **Orchestration** | Kubernetes | [1.29+] | Apache 2.0 | Auto-scaling; self-healing; portability |
| **Service mesh** | [Istio / Linkerd] | [version] | Apache 2.0 | mTLS; traffic management; observability |
| **API protocol (external)** | REST (OpenAPI 3.x) | — | — | Broad ecosystem; familiar to partners |
| **API protocol (internal)** | gRPC + Protobuf | [version] | Apache 2.0 | Strong typing; performance; streaming |
| **Message broker** | [Apache Kafka] | [3.x] | Apache 2.0 | Durable; ordered; replayable; high throughput |
| **Schema registry** | [Confluent Schema Registry] | [version] | Confluent Community | Avro schema versioning + compatibility checks |
| **RDBMS** | PostgreSQL | [16+] | PostgreSQL | ACID; rich feature set; JSONB; row-level security |
| **Document store** | [MongoDB] | [7.x] | SSPL | Flexible schema; [specific use case] |
| **Cache / sessions** | Redis | [7.x] | RSALv2 | Sub-ms latency; data structures; pub/sub |
| **Search** | [Elasticsearch / OpenSearch] | [8.x] | Apache 2.0 | Full-text; aggregations; geo |
| **Object storage** | [S3 / GCS] | — | — | Scalable; durable; event triggers |
| **Time-series** | [TimescaleDB / InfluxDB] | [version] | Apache 2.0 | Metrics; telemetry; IoT |
| **CDN** | [CloudFront / Cloudflare] | — | — | Global edge; DDoS; TLS |
| **API gateway** | [Kong / AWS API GW] | [version] | Apache 2.0 | Auth; rate limiting; routing |
| **Identity** | [Auth0 / Okta] | — | Commercial | Managed IdP; OIDC; MFA |
| **Secrets** | [HashiCorp Vault / AWS SM] | [version] | MPL / Commercial | Dynamic secrets; rotation; audit |
| **Observability** | [Grafana Stack / Datadog] | [version] | AGPL / Commercial | Metrics, logs, traces in one platform |
| **Tracing** | OpenTelemetry + [Jaeger / Tempo] | [version] | Apache 2.0 | Vendor-neutral; W3C TraceContext |
| **CI/CD** | [GitHub Actions / CircleCI] | — | — | Native Git integration; marketplace |
| **GitOps** | [ArgoCD / Flux] | [version] | Apache 2.0 | Declarative K8s delivery |

### 6.2 Deprecated / Prohibited Technologies

| Technology | Status | Reason | Migration Path |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Python 2.x] | Prohibited | End of life | Use Python 3.12+ |
| [jQuery] | Deprecated | Superseded by modern frameworks | Migrate to React / Vue |
| [HTTP (non-TLS)] | Prohibited | Security — plaintext traffic | Use HTTPS everywhere |
| [MD5 for passwords] | Prohibited | Insecure | Use bcrypt / argon2 |
| [FTP] | Prohibited | No encryption | Use SFTP / S3 |
| [Self-signed certs in prod] | Prohibited | Security risk | Use ACM / Let's Encrypt |
| [Mutable server configuration] | Prohibited | Non-reproducible | IaC only |

---

## 7. Application Architecture

### 7.1 Service Decomposition (C4 Level 2)

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Container Diagram — [System Name]

    Person(user, "End User")
    Person(ops, "Operator")

    System_Boundary(system, "[System Name]") {
        Container(spa, "Web SPA", "React 18", "Browser-based UI")
        Container(apigw, "API Gateway", "Kong", "Auth, rate limiting, routing")
        Container(svc_a, "[Service A]", "Go 1.22", "[Domain A] — [description]")
        Container(svc_b, "[Service B]", "Node.js 20", "[Domain B] — [description]")
```

## 8. API Design

### 8.1 REST API Standards

**URL design:**
```
Base URL:  https://api.[domain].com
Versioned: https://api.[domain].com/v{N}/{resource}

Examples:
  GET    /v1/users
  GET    /v1/users/{id}
  POST   /v1/users
  PATCH  /v1/users/{id}
  DELETE /v1/users/{id}
  GET    /v1/users/{id}/orders
  POST   /v1/users/{id}/orders
```

**Standard request headers:**

| Header | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Authorization` | Yes | `Bearer <JWT>` |
| `Content-Type` | Yes (with body) | `application/json` |
| `Accept` | No | `application/json` (default) |
| `X-Request-ID` | Recommended | Client-provided idempotency / correlation ID |
| `X-Idempotency-Key` | Required for POST | UUID — prevents duplicate mutations |
| `Accept-Language` | No | `en`, `fr`, etc. — for i18n responses |

**Standard response envelope:**
```json
{
  "data": { },
  "meta": {
    "request_id": "01HN1Z...",
    "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00.123Z",
    "api_version": "1.2.0"
  }
}
```

**Paginated list response:**
```json
{
  "data": [ ],
  "meta": {
    "request_id": "01HN1Z..."
  },
  "pagination": {
    "cursor": "eyJpZCI6MTAwfQ==",
    "has_more": true,
    "limit": 25,
    "total": 1042
  }
}
```

**Error response (RFC 7807):**
```json
{
  "type": "https://api.[domain].com/errors/validation-error",
  "title": "Validation Error",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "One or more fields failed validation.",
  "instance": "/v1/users",
  "request_id": "01HN1Z...",
  "errors": [
    {
      "field": "email",
      "code": "INVALID_FORMAT",
      "message": "Must be a valid email address."
    }
  ]
}
```

### 8.2 gRPC / Protobuf Standards

**Protobuf conventions:**
```protobuf
syntax = "proto3";

package com.[org].[service].v1;

option go_package = "github.com/[org]/[service]/gen/go/[service]/v1;[service]v1";

// UserService manages user lifecycle.
service UserService {
  rpc GetUser(GetUserRequest) returns (GetUserResponse);
  rpc ListUsers(ListUsersRequest) returns (ListUsersResponse);
  rpc CreateUser(CreateUserRequest) returns (CreateUserResponse);
  rpc UpdateUser(UpdateUserRequest) returns (UpdateUserResponse);
  rpc DeleteUser(DeleteUserRequest) returns (DeleteUserResponse);
  // Server-side streaming example
  rpc WatchUserEvents(WatchUserEventsRequest) returns (stream UserEvent);
}

message GetUserRequest {
  string user_id = 1; // UUID v4
}

message GetUserResponse {
  User user = 1;
}

message User {
  string id = 1;
  string email = 2;
  string full_name = 3;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp updated_at = 5;
}
```

**gRPC error codes mapping:**

| gRPC Code | HTTP Equivalent | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| `OK` | 200 | Success |
| `NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Resource doesn't exist |
| `ALREADY_EXISTS` | 409 | Duplicate resource |
| `INVALID_ARGUMENT` | 400 | Bad input |
| `UNAUTHENTICATED` | 401 | Missing / invalid auth |
| `PERMISSION_DENIED` | 403 | Auth ok, but not authorised |
| `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` | 429 | Rate limit exceeded |
| `UNAVAILABLE` | 503 | Service temporarily down |
| `INTERNAL` | 500 | Unexpected error |
| `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` | 504 | Timeout |

### 8.3 GraphQL (if applicable)

- **Used for:** [e.g., BFF layer for web portal; flexible client-driven queries]
- **Library:** [Apollo Server / Strawberry / gqlgen]
- **Schema-first:** SDL defined first; resolvers implement the schema
- **Query depth limit:** Max [5] levels deep — enforced by query complexity analysis
- **Rate limiting:** Points-based query complexity budget per consumer per minute
- **N+1 prevention:** DataLoader pattern on all list resolvers; mandatory for production
- **Persisted queries:** Enabled in production to reduce payload and prevent introspection abuse

### 8.4 Versioning Strategy

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Strategy["API Versioning"]
        V1[v1 — Current stable\nFull support] --> V2[v2 — New major version\nBreaking changes]
        V1 -->|Deprecated after v2 GA + [X] months| SUNSET[v1 Sunset\nSunset header set]
        SUNSET --> RETIRED[v1 Retired\nEndpoint removed]
    end
```

- **Non-breaking changes** (new optional fields, new endpoints): same version; changelog note
- **Breaking changes** (removed fields, changed types, renamed paths): new major version
- **Deprecation notice:** `Deprecation` + `Sunset` headers; changelog; direct consumer notification
- **Support window:** Minimum [X] months of overlap between major versions
- **Internal APIs (gRPC):** Protobuf package versioning (`v1`, `v2`); additive changes only within a version

## 9. Data Architecture

### 9.1 Database per Service Pattern

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph SvcA["Service A (Go)"]
        A_LOGIC[Business Logic]
    end
    subgraph SvcB["Service B (Node.js)"]
        B_LOGIC[Business Logic]
    end
    subgraph SvcC["Service C (Python)"]
        C_LOGIC[Business Logic]
    end

    A_LOGIC --> PG_A[(PostgreSQL\nschema: svc_a)]
    B_LOGIC --> MONGO_B[(MongoDB\ndb: svc_b)]
    C_LOGIC --> PG_C[(PostgreSQL\nschema: svc_c)]

    PG_A -.->|No direct cross-service\nDB access permitted| PG_C
```

**Rules:**
- Services access **only their own schema/database**
- Cross-service data access is via **API calls or events only** — never direct DB cross-access
- Schema ownership is tracked in [Data Catalog / service README]

### 9.2 Data Store Selection

| Service | Store | Version | Why This Store |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Service A] | PostgreSQL | 16 | ACID transactions; complex relational queries; JSONB for semi-structured data |
| [Service B] | MongoDB | 7.x | Flexible, evolving schema; document-oriented access patterns |
| [Service C] | PostgreSQL | 16 | Strong consistency; foreign keys; reporting queries |
| Sessions | Redis | 7.x | Sub-ms TTL-based lookups; volatile data |
| Search | Elasticsearch | 8.x | Full-text; faceted filtering; relevance scoring |
| Files/Media | S3 / GCS | — | Durable; unlimited scale; CDN integration |
| Metrics | TimescaleDB | 2.x | Time-series queries; downsampling; retention |
| Events | Kafka | 3.x | Durable ordered log; replay; high throughput |

### 9.3 PostgreSQL Configuration & Access Patterns

**Connection management:**

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Connection pooler | PgBouncer | Prevent connection exhaustion; pool mode: transaction |
| Max DB connections | [X] | Instance size dependent — [instance type] |
| Pool size per service | [X] | Sized per peak RPS × avg query duration |
| Connection timeout | [X]ms | Fail fast if pool exhausted |
| Statement timeout | [X]s | Kill runaway queries |
| Lock timeout | [X]s | Prevent lock queue buildup |
| `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout` | [X]s | Kill abandoned transactions |

**Schema management:**

```
migrations/
├── V001__create_users_table.sql
├── V002__add_users_email_index.sql
├── V003__create_orders_table.sql
└── V004__add_orders_status_index.sql
```

- **Tool:** [Flyway / Liquibase / golang-migrate / Alembic]
- **Strategy:** Forward-only versioned migrations; no `down` migrations in production
- **Review gate:** Schema changes affecting > 1 service require architecture review
- **Zero-downtime migrations:** Expand-contract pattern (add column → backfill → switch → drop old)

**Indexing standards:**
```sql
-- Naming: idx_{table}_{columns}
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_customer_id ON orders(customer_id);
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_status_created_at ON orders(status, created_at DESC);

-- Partial indexes for common filtered queries
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_pending
  ON orders(created_at DESC)
  WHERE status = 'pending';

-- Expression index for case-insensitive search
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_users_email_lower
  ON users(lower(email));
```

### 9.4 MongoDB Configuration & Patterns

- **Driver:** [mongo-go-driver / Mongoose / pymongo]
- **Connection string:** Replica set URI — never single node in production
- **Read preference:** `primaryPreferred` for most reads; `secondaryPreferred` for analytics
- **Write concern:** `{ w: "majority", j: true }` for critical writes
- **Schema validation:** JSON Schema validation at collection level for all critical collections
- **Indexing:** All query fields indexed; compound index order: equality → sort → range
- **Document size limit:** [X]KB soft limit per document — large payloads in S3

### 9.5 Redis Usage Patterns

| Use Case | Key Pattern | TTL | Data Structure |
|---|---|---|---|
| User session | `session:{session_id}` | 24h (sliding) | Hash |
| Rate limit counter | `ratelimit:{consumer_id}:{window}` | 60s | String (INCR) |
| Cache: user profile | `cache:user:{user_id}` | 5min | Hash / String |
| Cache: API response | `cache:api:{route_hash}` | [X]min | String (JSON) |
| Distributed lock | `lock:{resource_id}` | [X]s | String (SET NX PX) |
| Pub/Sub channel | `events:{service}:{event_type}` | — | Pub/Sub |
| Job queue | `queue:{job_type}` | — | List (LPUSH/BRPOP) |
| Idempotency key | `idempotency:{key}` | 24h | String (JSON response) |

**Redis configuration:**
- **Mode:** Redis Cluster (production); Single node (dev/test)
- **Eviction policy:** `allkeys-lru` — evict least-recently-used when memory full
- **Max memory:** [X]GB — set below instance memory to prevent OOM
- **Persistence:** AOF with `appendfsync everysec` for non-cache data; none for pure cache
- **TLS:** Enabled in all environments above dev

### 9.6 Data Migration & Evolution

**Zero-downtime migration pattern (Expand-Contract):**

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    S1["Step 1: Expand\nAdd new column (nullable)\nDeploy code that writes\nboth old + new column"]
    S2["Step 2: Backfill\nBackground job fills\nnew column for existing rows"]
    S3["Step 3: Verify\nConfirm 100% of rows\nhave new column populated"]
    S4["Step 4: Switch\nDeploy code that reads\nfrom new column only"]
    S5["Step 5: Contract\nDrop old column in\nnext migration cycle"]

    S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> S4 --> S5
```

## 10. Concurrency & Asynchronous Processing

### 10.1 Concurrency Model by Runtime

| Runtime | Model | Primitives | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Go | Goroutines + channels | `go`, `chan`, `sync.WaitGroup`, `errgroup` | M:N threading; lightweight goroutines; prefer channels over shared memory |
| Node.js | Event loop + async/await | `Promise`, `async/await`, worker threads | Single-threaded event loop; CPU-bound work in worker threads |
| Python | asyncio / threading / multiprocessing | `asyncio`, `ThreadPoolExecutor`, `multiprocessing` | GIL limits threading for CPU-bound; asyncio for I/O-bound |

### 10.2 Go Concurrency Patterns

```go
// Pattern 1: Worker pool with bounded concurrency
func processItems(ctx context.Context, items []Item) error {
    g, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)
    sem := make(chan struct{}, maxWorkers) // bounded semaphore

    for _, item := range items {
        item := item // capture loop var
        g.Go(func() error {
            sem <- struct{}{}
            defer func() { <-sem }()
            return processItem(ctx, item)
        })
    }
    return g.Wait()
}

// Pattern 2: Context-aware cancellation
func fetchWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, url string) ([]byte, error) {
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second)
    defer cancel()
    // ... HTTP call respects ctx cancellation
}
```

### 10.3 Async Event Processing Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant P as Producer Service
    participant K as Kafka Topic
    participant W as Worker (Consumer)
    participant DB as Database
    participant DLQ as Dead Letter Topic

    P->>K: Publish event (Avro encoded)
    K->>W: Deliver to consumer group
    W->>W: Deserialise + validate schema
    alt Processing succeeds
        W->>DB: Write result
        W->>K: Commit offset
    else Retryable error (< max retries)
        W->>W: Exponential backoff + retry
    else Non-retryable or max retries exceeded
        W->>DLQ: Publish to DLQ with error metadata
        W->>K: Commit offset (skip poison pill)
    end
```

### 10.4 Async Standards

- **Max goroutines / threads per service:** [X] — enforced via bounded worker pool
- **Context propagation:** `context.Context` passed as first arg to all functions making I/O calls
- **Graceful shutdown:** All services handle `SIGTERM`; drain in-flight requests before exit; max drain time [X]s
- **Kafka consumer:** Auto-commit disabled; manual commit after successful processing only
- **Message ordering:** Preserved per Kafka partition key; choose partition key carefully (e.g., `customer_id`)

## 11. Caching Architecture

### 11.1 Cache Layers

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    CLIENT[Client Request]

    CLIENT --> CDN_C[CDN Cache\nEdge — CloudFront/Fastly\nStatic assets, public API responses]
    CDN_C -- miss --> GW_C[API Gateway Cache\nResponse cache plugin\nHeavy read, slow-change endpoints]
    GW_C -- miss --> APP_C[Application Cache\nRedis\nUser profiles, hot data, rate limits]
    APP_C -- miss --> DB_C[(Database\nSource of truth)]
    DB_C --> APP_C
    APP_C --> GW_C
    GW_C --> CDN_C
    CDN_C --> CLIENT
```

### 11.2 Cache Strategy by Data Type

| Data | Store | Strategy | TTL | Invalidation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Static assets (JS, CSS, images) | CDN | Cache-Control: max-age=31536000, immutable | 1 year | Content-hash filename |
| Public API responses (read-heavy) | API Gateway + Redis | Cache-aside | [X]min | TTL + manual purge on mutation |
| User profile | Redis | Write-through | [X]min | Invalidated on profile update event |
| Permissions / RBAC rules | Redis | Read-through | [X]min | Invalidated on role change event |
| Rate limit counter | Redis | In-place (INCR) | Per window (60s) | Automatic TTL expiry |
| Search facet counts | Redis | Cache-aside | [X]min | TTL |
| Database query results | Redis | Cache-aside | [X]s | TTL; invalidated on table write |
| Feature flags | In-process (memory) | Background refresh | [X]s | Polled from config service |
| Session tokens | Redis | Write-through (on login) | 24h (sliding) | Explicit delete on logout |

### 11.3 Cache Consistency Patterns

| Pattern | Description | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| **Cache-aside (lazy loading)** | Read: check cache → miss → DB → populate cache | Default for most use cases |
| **Write-through** | Write: update DB → update cache atomically | When stale reads are unacceptable |
| **Write-behind (write-back)** | Write: update cache → async write to DB | High-write, lower consistency tolerance |
| **Read-through** | Cache handles DB miss transparently | Libraries that support it (Caffeine, etc.) |
| **Refresh-ahead** | Background refresh before TTL expires | Eliminates cache-miss latency spikes |

### 11.4 Cache Stampede Prevention

```go
// Single-flight pattern — collapse concurrent cache misses into one DB call
var group singleflight.Group

func getUserProfile(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*Profile, error) {
    key := "profile:" + userID
    result, err, _ := group.Do(key, func() (interface{}, error) {
        // Only one goroutine executes this for the same key
        profile, err := db.GetUser(ctx, userID)
        if err != nil {
            return nil, err
        }
        cache.Set(ctx, key, profile, 5*time.Minute)
        return profile, nil
    })
    // ...
}
```
