## 22. Testing Strategy

### 22.1 Testing Approach

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph TestPyramid["Test Approach"]
        E2E_TS[E2E Tests\nFull user journeys\nPlays wright / Cypress\nSlowest · fewest]
        CONTRACT_TS[Contract Tests\nAPI consumer/producer\nPact]
        INTEGRATION_TS[Integration Tests\nService + real dependencies\ntestcontainers]
        UNIT_TS[Unit Tests\nBusiness logic\nFastest · most]
    end
    UNIT_TS --> INTEGRATION_TS --> CONTRACT_TS --> E2E_TS
```

### 22.2 Test Coverage Plan

| Test Type | What Is Tested | Tool | Coverage Target | Blocks Deploy? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Unit | Business logic, domain rules, data mappers | [Jest / Go test / pytest] | > [X]% on domain code | Yes — PR gate |
| Integration | Service + DB + cache (real, via testcontainers) | [Go test / Supertest] | All happy + key error paths | Yes — PR gate |
| Contract | API request/response shape between services | [Pact] | 100% of consumer/producer pairs | Yes — deploy gate |
| E2E | Top [X] user journeys end-to-end | [Playwright / Cypress] | [X] critical journeys | Yes — prod gate |
| Load | p99 latency + error rate at [X] RPS | [k6] | NFR thresholds met | Advisory (weekly) |
| Security (DAST) | API attack surface (OWASP Top 10) | [OWASP ZAP] | OWASP Top 10 | Yes — prod gate |
| Chaos | Failure injection (kill pod, network partition) | [Chaos Mesh] | Key failure modes | Advisory (staging) |

### 22.3 Definition of Done

A feature is considered done when:
- [ ] Unit tests pass with > [X]% coverage on new code
- [ ] Integration tests cover happy path + at least 2 error paths
- [ ] Contract tests updated and passing for all affected APIs
- [ ] Linter + formatter pass with zero warnings
- [ ] Security scan: no new high/critical findings
- [ ] Observability: structured logs, metrics, and trace spans present
- [ ] Runbook updated for any new alert
- [ ] API spec (OpenAPI / AsyncAPI) updated if API changed
- [ ] Feature flag added for any user-visible feature
- [ ] Performance tested if the change is in a critical path

## 27. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| ADR | Architecture Decision Record — documents significant design decisions and their rationale |
| BFF | Backend for Frontend — API aggregation layer tailored to a specific client type |
| Big Bang migration | Cutover from old to new system in a single event |
| CDC | Change Data Capture — captures row-level database changes as a stream |
| Circuit Breaker | Pattern that stops calling a failing downstream after an error threshold |
| C4 | Context, Container, Component, Code — architectural diagramming model |
| CloudEvents | CNCF specification for event envelope format |
| CQRS | Command Query Responsibility Segregation — separate read/write models |
| DLQ | Dead Letter Queue — destination for messages that failed max retry attempts |
| Domain event | A record of something that happened within a bounded domain |
| Expand-Contract | Zero-downtime database migration pattern |
| Graceful degradation | System continues operating in reduced capacity when a dependency fails |
| HLD | High Level Design — this document |
| HPA | Horizontal Pod Autoscaler — K8s mechanism for scaling based on metrics |
| Idempotency | Property where an operation produces the same result when repeated |
| IaC | Infrastructure as Code |
| JWT | JSON Web Token |
| KEDA | Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaling |
| LCP | Largest Contentful Paint — Core Web Vitals loading metric |
| mTLS | Mutual TLS — both parties authenticate via certificate |
| NFR | Non-Functional Requirement |
| OIDC | OpenID Connect — identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 |
| PII | Personally Identifiable Information |
| RBAC | Role-Based Access Control |
| RFC 7807 | IETF standard for HTTP API error response format |
| RPO | Recovery Point Objective — maximum acceptable data loss |
| RTO | Recovery Time Objective — maximum acceptable downtime |
| SLA | Service Level Agreement |
| SLI | Service Level Indicator — a measurable metric |
| SLO | Service Level Objective — target value for an SLI |
| SPA | Single Page Application |
| Strangler Fig | Pattern for incrementally replacing a legacy system |
| WCAG | Web Content Accessibility Guidelines |
| [Term] | [Definition] |

### Appendix A: HLD Review Checklist

> Use during design review sessions to ensure completeness.

**Problem & scope:**
- [ ] Problem statement is clear and quantified
- [ ] Goals are specific and measurable
- [ ] Non-goals explicitly stated
- [ ] Assumptions documented and owners assigned

**Design:**
- [ ] At least 2 options evaluated with explicit trade-offs
- [ ] Recommended option justified with evidence
- [ ] Context diagram shows all external systems
- [ ] Component responsibilities are clearly defined
- [ ] Database-per-service principle followed
- [ ] All integrations listed with direction, pattern, and owner

**Quality attributes:**
- [ ] NFRs defined and traceable to design decisions
- [ ] Caching strategy addresses latency targets
- [ ] Failure modes documented with graceful degradation behaviour
- [ ] SLOs defined for user-facing features
- [ ] Security controls cover all 7 layers (perimeter → audit)

**Delivery:**
- [ ] Phased rollout plan with exit criteria per phase
- [ ] Rollback plan exists for each phase
- [ ] Open questions have owners and target resolution dates
- [ ] All dependencies identified with owners and statuses
- [ ] Top [X] risks documented with mitigations
